  filing_date=2026-02-13 section=business period_end=2025-12-31
Item 1. Business.
Overview
JPMorgan Chase & Co. ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm", NYSE: JPM), a financial holding company incorporated under Delaware law in 1968, is a leading financial services firm based in the United States of America ("U.S."), with operations worldwide. JPMorganChase had $4.4 trillion in assets and $362.4 billion in stockholders' equity as of December 31, 2025. The Firm is a leader in investment banking, financial services for consumers and small businesses, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management. Under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands, the Firm serves millions of customers, predominantly in the U.S., and many of the world's most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients globally.
JPMorganChase's principal bank subsidiary is JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association ("JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A."), a national banking association with U.S. branches in 48 states and Washington, D.C. JPMorganChase's principal non-bank subsidiary is J.P. Morgan Securities LLC ("J.P. Morgan Securities"), a U.S. broker-dealer. The bank and non-bank subsidiaries of JPMorganChase operate nationally as well as through overseas branches and subsidiaries, representative offices and subsidiary foreign banks. The Firm's principal operating subsidiaries outside the U.S. are J.P. Morgan Securities plc and J.P. Morgan SE ("JPMSE"), which are subsidiaries of JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. and are based in the United Kingdom ("U.K.") and Germany, respectively.
The Firm's website is www.jpmorganchase.com. JPMorganChase makes available on its website, free of charge, annual reports on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K pursuant to Section 13(a) or Section 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as soon as reasonably practicable after it electronically files or furnishes such material to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") at www.sec.gov. JPMorganChase makes new and important information about the Firm available on its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com, including on the Investor Relations section of its website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir. Information on the Firm's website, including documents on the website that are referenced in this Form 10-K, is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025 ("2025 Form 10-K" or "Form 10-K") or the Firm's other filings with the SEC.
Business segments & Corporate
For management reporting purposes, the Firm has three reportable business segments – Consumer & Community Banking ("CCB"), Commercial & Investment Bank ("CIB") and Asset & Wealth Management ("AWM") – with the remaining activities in Corporate. The Firm's consumer business segment is CCB, and the Firm's wholesale business segments are CIB and AWM.
A description of the Firm's reportable business segments and the products and services that they provide to their respective client bases, as well as a description of Corporate activities, is provided in the Management's discussion and analysis of financial condition and results of operations section of this Form 10-K ("Management's discussion and analysis" or "MD&A") under the heading "Business Segment & Corporate Results," which begins on page 46, and in Note 32.
Competition
JPMorganChase and its subsidiaries and affiliates operate in highly competitive environments. Competitors include other banks, brokerage firms, investment banking companies, merchant banks, hedge funds, commodity trading companies, private equity firms, insurance companies, mutual fund companies, investment managers, credit card companies, mortgage banking companies, trust companies, securities processing companies, automobile financing companies, leasing companies, e-commerce and other internet-based companies, digital asset and other financial technology companies, and other companies engaged in providing similar and new products and services. The Firm's businesses generally compete on the basis of the quality and variety of the Firm's products and services, transaction execution, innovation, reputation and price. Competition also varies based on the types of clients, customers, industries and geographies served. With respect to some of its geographies and products, JPMorganChase competes globally; with respect to others, the Firm competes on a national or regional basis. New competitors in the financial services industry continue to emerge, including firms that offer products and services solely through the internet and non-financial companies that offer products and services that disintermediate traditional banking products and services offered by financial services firms such as JPMorganChase.

**Quantitative Report: JPM (as of 2026-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Quarterly revenue for Q4 2025 (period_end 2025-12-31) was $45.80B, with diluted EPS of $4.64. The quarterly trajectory shows sequential revenue decline of -1.4% from Q3's $46.43B and EPS decline of -8.5% from Q3's $5.07. TTM revenue stands at $182.45B with net income of $57.05B.
*   **Margins:** Operating margin for Q4 2025 was 37.5% (operating income $17.16B / revenue $45.80B). The latest reported net margin (Q4 2025) is 0.313.
*   **Cash Flow:** Quarterly and trailing cash flow data is unavailable for all supplied periods.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets were $4,424.90B with total equity of $362.44B as of Q4 2025. Equity has grown steadily from $336.64B in Q1 2024.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** Assessment is limited. The reported net margin of 0.313 is stable across the provided valuation history. The QoQ EPS decline of -8.5% represents a notable earnings dip, but the absence of cash flow statement data prevents analysis of cash conversion or accruals.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Total liabilities were $4,062.46B against total equity of $362.44B, implying a high leverage ratio (Total Assets/Total Equity) of 12.2x as of Q4 2025, which is characteristic of a bank. Equity has increased sequentially each quarter from $336.64B (Q1 2024) to $362.44B (Q4 2025).

**3. Valuation versus Company's Own Supplied History**
*   As of 2026-03-31, the diluted P/E ratio is 14.693. Over the supplied 12-point valuation history from 2025-04-01 to 2026-03-31, this is below the peak of 16.906 (observed 2025-12-19) and above the trough of 12.986 (observed 2025-04-01).
*   The Price/Book ratio is 2.258, within its supplied 1-year range of 1.7 to 2.8 and below its recent high of 2.629 (2025-12-19).
*   Earnings yield is 0.068. The net margin (0.313) and ROE (0.157) are stable at their latest recorded levels.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
*   The closing price on 2026-03-31 is $294.16. The stock is down -9.1% over the prior quarter (~63 trading days from 2025-12-29 close of $323.75) but up +21.1% over the prior year (~252 trading days from 2025-03-28 close of $242.85).
*   The 52-week range is $202.16 to $337.25, placing the current price in the upper half of this range.
*   The recent quarterly price decline of -9.1% coincides with the reported sequential decline in Q4 2025 EPS (-8.5%) and revenue (-1.4%). The annual price return of +21.1% is supported by TTM net income of $57.05B and expanding equity.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
*   **Upside:** The stock is trading at a P/E of 14.693, which is below the midpoint of its 365-day range (11.2..17.8) and significantly below its recent peak P/E of 16.906. A reversion of the quarterly earnings decline towards the higher EPS levels seen in Q2 2025 ($5.24) and Q3 2025 ($5.07) could provide a catalyst.
*   **Downside:** The recent -8.5% QoQ EPS decline and -1.4% revenue decline indicate a potential negative earnings trajectory. The high leverage (12.2x assets/equity) amplifies sensitivity to asset quality or interest rate shifts, though specific risk metrics are not provided. The absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of dividend or capital return sustainability.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative picture presents a mixed risk/reward. The stock's valuation (P/E 14.69) has compressed from recent highs but is not at the low end of its historical range. The recent fundamental trajectory is negative on a quarterly basis (EPS -8.5% QoQ), though TTM earnings remain substantial at $57.05B. Balance sheet equity is growing, but high bank leverage introduces structural risk that is unquantifiable without detailed capital or cash flow metrics. The -9.1% quarterly price decline has aligned with the earnings dip, suggesting the move is fundamental rather than purely sentiment-driven. Current valuation is neutral relative to its own history, pending clarity on whether the Q4 earnings decline is an isolated event or the start of a trend.
JPM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM)**
**Date of Analysis:** 2026-04-01
**Evidence Cut-off:** 2026-03-31

---

### 1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative
The 10-K filing (2026-02-13) describes JPMorgan Chase as a leading, globally diversified financial services firm. As of 2025-12-31, it held $4.4 trillion in assets and $362.4 billion in stockholders' equity. Its core operations are conducted through three segments: Consumer & Community Banking (CCB), Commercial & Investment Bank (CIB), and Asset & Wealth Management (AWM). The firm is a leader in investment banking, consumer/small business banking, commercial banking, transaction processing, and asset management. It operates under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands across the U.S. (48 states and D.C.) and internationally. The firm operates in a highly competitive environment against traditional banks, fintechs, and other financial and non-financial companies.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **2026-02-24/2026-02-27:** In a late-February investor update, JPMorgan Chase provided an optimistic outlook, **expecting strong growth in first-quarter investment banking fees and markets revenue**. It **nudged up its firmwide 2026 net interest income (NII) forecast to approximately $104.5 billion** (including markets revenue).
*   **2026-02-17:** A report noted JPMorgan's **2026 NII guidance of $103 billion**, suggesting a plateauing of interest income, and identified the **integration of the Apple Card and deployment of AI initiatives** as primary metrics for success.
*   **2026-03-25:** An analysis highlighted that JPMorgan Chase **closed 2025 with $57 billion in net income and a 20% ROTCE**, but pointed to the fading of rate tailwinds as a key factor for 2026 performance. It also stated the bank **plans to spend $105 billion** (context: likely refers to a multi-year tech/spend plan).
*   **2026-03-25:** A news article stated that JPMorgan's **15.3% CET1 ratio and $100 billion+ NII guidance suggest it is better positioned than almost any other financial institution in the world.**
*   **Ongoing Legal Actions:**
    *   **2026-02-13:** A U.S. judge ruled JPMorgan must face part of a class action lawsuit over near-zero interest rates on cash sweeps in brokerage and retirement accounts.
    *   **2026-02-19/2026-03-25:** JPMorgan is fighting a lawsuit filed by former President Trump alleging the bank "debanked" his accounts for political reasons; the bank is seeking to dismiss the suit.
    *   **2026-03-09:** A federal judge ruled JPMorgan employees may pursue part of a lawsuit accusing the bank of mismanaging its health and prescription drug plan.
    *   **2026-03-02:** JPMorgan is facing a lawsuit alleging it enabled fraud tied to bankrupt auto lender Tricolor.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Macroeconomic & Market Headwinds:** The supplied news indicates a challenging 2026 environment with **rising inflation, recession concerns, elevated oil prices ($100/barrel), and rising interest rates** (2026-03-27, 2026-03-28). Market breadth is deteriorating, and major indexes were on track for their worst month in years (2026-03-31, 2026-03-30).
*   **Legal and Reputational Exposure:** The firm is managing **multiple, concurrent lawsuits** related to cash sweep rates, employee health plans, alleged fraud facilitation, and a politically charged "debanking" case (2026-02-13, 2026-02-19, 2026-03-02, 2026-03-09, 2026-03-25). These represent potential financial and reputational liabilities.
*   **Rate Environment Shift:** Analysis suggests the **tailwind from rising rates that boosted net interest income is fading**, which is a structural headwind for 2026 performance (2026-03-25).
*   **Intense Competition:** The 10-K explicitly details a highly competitive landscape including traditional peers, fintechs, and disintermediating non-financial firms (2026-02-13).

**Offsets:**
*   **Strong Capital & Profitability Position:** The firm is described as having a **strong CET1 ratio (15.3%)** and concluded 2025 with record profits (**$57B net income, 20% ROTCE**) (2026-03-25).
*   **Resilient Revenue Outlook:** Despite macro headwinds, the bank's own guidance projects **robust NII (>$100B)** and **strength in investment banking and markets revenue** for early 2026 (2026-02-24, 2026-02-27).
*   **Strategic Investments:** The firm is actively pursuing growth through **technology/AI initiatives and integration of new products (Apple Card)** (2026-02-17) alongside a major, multi-year spending plan.

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Cautious Positive.**
The assessment is anchored by JPM's confirmed **dominant franchise scale, fortress balance sheet (high CET1), and management's own guidance for resilient revenue streams in 2026**, particularly in its investment bank. This provides a substantial buffer against the documented **clear and present macro risks (inflation, recession, rates) and legal overhangs**. The firm's proactive strategic spending suggests confidence in its long-term positioning. The key near-term factor is whether the strong capital markets performance and NII resilience cited in the February guidance can materialize amidst the market volatility and geopolitical tension (Iran) reported in March. The legal risks are manageable but warrant monitoring.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: JPMorgan Chase presents a balanced risk/reward profile: a dominant franchise with fortress capital (15.3% CET1, $57B net income, 20% ROTCE) and resilient revenue guidance ($104.5B NII) is offset by a fading rate tailwind, a recent -8.5% QoQ EPS decline, and a challenging macro environment with recession concerns and rising rates. The stock's P/E of 14.7 is mid-range relative to its own history, offering neither compelling value nor clear overvaluation.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: JPMorgan Chase presents a balanced risk/reward profile: a dominant franchise with fortress capital (15.3% CET1, $57B net income, 20% ROTCE) and resilient revenue guidance ($104.5B NII) is offset by a fading rate tailwind, a recent -8.5% QoQ EPS decline, and a challenging macro environment with recession concerns and rising rates. The stock's P/E of 14.7 is mid-range relative to its own history, offering neither compelling value nor clear overvaluation.

Financing Arrangements
We provide financing arrangements for certain qualified customers to build, maintain, and upgrade their networks. We believe customer financing is a competitive advantage in obtaining business, particularly for those customers involved in significant infrastructure projects. Our financing arrangements include loans, leases (sales-type, direct financing and operating) and channel financing arrangements.
Acquisitions, Investments, and Alliances
The markets in which we compete require a wide variety of technologies, products, and capabilities. We continue to evaluate opportunities to acquire and invest in businesses and technologies that complement and enable further investment in our key priority areas.
5
Table of Contents
Acquisitions
We acquire companies in order to gain access to talent, technology, products and features, operational capabilities or new markets. The risks associated with acquisitions are more fully discussed in "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factor entitled "We have made and expect to continue to make acquisitions that could disrupt our operations and harm our operating results."
Investments in Privately Held Companies
We make investments in privately held companies that develop technology or provide services that are complementary to our products or that provide insights into emerging technologies that may become relevant to our businesses. The risks associated with these investments are more fully discussed in "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factor entitled "We are exposed to fluctuations in the market values of our portfolio investments and in interest rates; impairment of our investments could harm our earnings."
Strategic Alliances
We pursue strategic alliances with other companies in areas where collaboration can produce industry advancement and accelerate new markets. The objectives and goals of a strategic alliance can include one or more of the following: technology exchange, product development, joint sales and marketing, or new market creation.
The risks associated with our strategic alliances are more fully discussed in "Item 1A. Risk Factors," including the risk factor entitled "If we do not successfully manage our strategic alliances, we may not realize the expected benefits from such alliances and we may experience increased competition or delays in product development."
Competition
We compete in the networking and communications equipment markets, providing products and services designed to transport, and help secure data, voice, and video traffic across cloud, private and public networks and the Internet. We compete with numerous vendors in each product category. The overall number of our competitors providing niche product solutions may increase. Also, the identity and composition of competitors may change as we increase our activity in newer product areas, and in key priority areas. As we continue to expand globally, we may see new competition in different geographic regions. In particular, we have experienced price-focused competition from competitors in Asia, especially from China, and we anticipate this will continue.
Our competitors (in each case relative to only some of our products or services) include: Amazon Web Services LLC; Arista Networks, Inc.; Broadcom Inc.; Ciena Corporation; CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.; Datadog Inc.; Dell Technologies Inc.; Dynatrace Inc.; Fortinet, Inc.; Hewlett-Packard Enterprise Company; Huawei Technologies Co., Ltd.; Microsoft Corporation; New Relic, Inc.; Nokia Corporation; Nvidia Corporation; Palo Alto Networks, Inc.; RingCentral, Inc.; Zoom Video Communications, Inc.; and Zscaler, Inc.; among others.
Some of our competitors compete across many of our product lines, while others are primarily focused in a specific product area. Barriers to entry are relatively low, and new ventures to create products that do or could compete with our products are regularly formed. In addition, some of our competitors may have greater resources, including technical and engineering resources, than we do. As we expand into new markets, we will face competition not only from our existing competitors but also from other competitors, including existing companies with strong technological, marketing, and sales positions in those markets. We also sometimes face competition from resellers and distributors of our products. Companies with which we have strategic alliances in some areas may be competitors in other areas, and this trend may increase. For example, the enterprise data center is undergoing a fundamental transformation arising from the convergence of technologies, including computing, networking, storage, and software, that previously were segregated. Additionally, companies that are strategic alliance partners in some areas of our business may acquire or form alliances with our competitors, thereby reducing their business with us.
The principal competitive factors in the markets in which we presently compete and may compete in the future include the ability to sell successful business outcomes; the ability to provide a broad range of networking and communications products and services; product performance; price; the ability to introduce new products, including providing continuous new customer value and products with price-performance advantages; the ability to reduce production costs; the ability to provide value-added features such as security, reliability, and investment protection; conformance to standards; market presence; the ability to provide financing; and disruptive technology shifts and new business models.
We also face competition from customers to which we license or supply technology and suppliers from which we transfer technology. The inherent nature of networking requires interoperability. As such, we must cooperate and, at the same time, compete with many companies. **Quantitative Assessment: CSCO (2026-04-01)**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue & Earnings Growth:** Consistent quarterly revenue growth from $12.70B (2024-04-27) to $15.35B (2026-01-24). Latest YoY revenue growth of +9.7%, YoY EPS growth of +31.1%. TTM revenue is $59.05B with TTM net income of $11.08B.
- **Margins:** Gross margin stable at ~64.8% (latest: 65.1% of revenue). Operating margin improved to 24.6% (latest: 24.6%). Net margin expanded from 14.9% (2024-04-27) to 20.7% (latest).
- **Balance Sheet:** Total equity grew from $45.77B to $47.72B over eight quarters. Total debt increased from $20.10B to $24.60B. Debt-to-equity ratio is 0.515. Total assets stable around $123B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Net income growth (YoY +30.8%) outpaces revenue growth, supported by stable gross margin and expanding operating margin. No cash flow data supplied; inability to assess cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Equity base of $47.72B supports debt load. Debt-to-equity ratio of 0.515 is moderate. Total liabilities ($75.65B) are 61% of total assets ($123.37B).

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- **P/E:** Latest P/E (diluted) is 27.909, within its 1-year range of 23.2 to 33.5.
- **P/B:** Latest P/B is 6.477, within its 1-year range of 4.7 to 7.4.
- **Profitability Ratios:** Net margin (0.188) and ROE (0.232) have improved from 0.17 and 0.202, respectively, since April 2025.
- **Earnings Yield:** Current earnings yield is 3.6% (1/27.909).

**4. Price Level & Returns Context**
- **Current Price:** $77.59 (2026-03-31).
- **Recent Performance:** Quarterly return is -0.3%. Annual return is +27.5%. The stock is trading near the midpoint of its 52-week range ($52.11 to $88.19).
- **Fundamental vs. Price Path:** The +27.5% annual price return aligns with strong YoY earnings growth (+31.1%) and expanding margins. Recent flat quarterly performance (-0.3%) contrasts with continued quarterly revenue growth (+3.1%).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Continuation of revenue growth (+9.7% YoY) combined with margin expansion (net margin from 0.179 to 0.188) could drive further earnings growth. ROE improvement to 0.232 enhances shareholder value creation.
- **Downside:** Valuation multiples are at mid-to-upper historical ranges (P/E 27.9 vs. range low 23.2). Without supplied cash flow data, the quality of reported earnings cannot be verified against cash generation. Debt has increased by $4.5B over the period.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- **Conclusion:** Fundamental trajectory is positive with strong revenue growth, margin expansion, and rising ROE. The balance sheet remains resilient. However, the valuation is not low relative to its own recent history (P/E 27.9, mid-range), and the recent price stagnation (+0.3% quarterly) suggests the market may be pricing in the improved fundamentals. The absence of cash flow data is a key gap in assessing earnings quality. Quantitative risk/reward appears **neutral to marginally positive**, contingent on sustained growth to justify the valuation and clarification of cash generation.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Cisco Systems (CSCO)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2025-09-03)**
Cisco is a global technology leader designing and selling a broad range of networking, security, collaboration, and observability products and services. Its strategy centers on "securely connecting everything" to address three key customer priorities: building modern infrastructure, enhancing cybersecurity, and harnessing AI and data. The company is integrating AI across its portfolio and focuses on delivering "One Cisco" outcomes: AI-ready data centers, future-proofed workplaces, and digital resilience. Its customer base spans enterprises, public institutions, and service providers, including large webscale providers. The business is organized into three geographic segments: Americas, EMEA, and APJC.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)**
*   **Strategic AI Partnership (2026-03-17):** Cisco and Nvidia expanded their "Secure AI Factory" platform to help enterprises deploy and secure AI across data centers and edge environments, aiming to reduce deployment timelines from months to weeks.
*   **Positive Sector Sentiment (2026-03-24):** Nokia's stock rose on 5G momentum and North American telecom spending, with the article noting that peer companies Ericsson and Cisco also posted gains, indicating positive sector-wide sentiment.
*   **Market Tailwinds (2026-03-10):** Industry reports project strong growth in markets relevant to Cisco's core networking business, including the U.S. optical interconnect market (CAGR ~14% through 2035) and the U.S. Wi-Fi 6 market (projected to reach $35-84 billion by 2035).
*   **Analyst Sentiment & Cost Concerns (2026-02-12, 2026-02-20):** Wall Street reaction to recent earnings was mixed; some viewed results as "solid" (2026-02-12), while others remained bullish on strong demand and earnings (2026-02-20). However, rising memory costs were cited as a concern for investors (2026-02-12).
*   **Stock Performance (2026-03-17):** Despite the positive AI partnership news, Cisco shares were down 0.71% in premarket trading, with the article noting mixed technical signals.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Legal and Reputational Exposure.** Supplied text references ongoing litigation, including a case alleging Cisco executives developed and sold surveillance technology to the Chinese government used against Falun Gong practitioners (2026-03-27). This poses potential reputational and legal risk.
*   **Risk: Intense Competition.** The 10-K (2025-09-03) explicitly details competition from numerous vendors across all product categories, including large, well-resourced companies like Arista, Palo Alto Networks, and Nvidia. The text notes barriers to entry are relatively low and competition from Asia, especially China, is expected to continue.
*   **Risk: Input Cost Inflation.** Recent narrative highlights investor concern over rising memory costs impacting margins (2026-02-12).
*   **Offset: Strong Position in Growing Markets.** Cisco is strategically positioned in high-growth areas like AI infrastructure, cybersecurity, and observability. The 10-K emphasizes its integrated portfolio and the strategic acquisition of Splunk to bolster security analytics.
*   **Offset: Strategic Alliances and AI Focus.** The expanded partnership with Nvidia (2026-03-17) and the company's stated focus on embedding AI across its portfolio demonstrate active efforts to capture the AI infrastructure build-out.
*   **Offset: Diversified Customer Base and Recurring Revenue.** The business model serves a wide range of customers (enterprise, public sector, service providers) and is increasingly shifting toward software and subscriptions, as indicated by the 10-K's description of SaaS and software license offerings.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** The evidence presents a balanced picture. Cisco's core franchise in networking and security is solid, and it is making strategic moves to capitalize on the secular AI trend through partnerships and product integration. Positive sector trends in 5G and networking infrastructure provide a favorable backdrop. However, these positives are counterbalanced by clear and present risks, including intense competition, potential cost pressures, and material legal/reputational challenges. The recent stock performance, down on a day of positive company-specific news, suggests the market may be weighing these risks heavily. The qualitative outlook is therefore balanced between strategic opportunity and operational/competitive headwinds.

  2026-03-12 — Apple Risk Factors: A Plain-Eng
…[truncated 9643 chars]…
es and accessories. Therefore, many components used by the Company, including those that are available from multiple sources, are at times subject to industry-wide shortage and significant commodity pricing fluctuations. Restrictions on international trade can increase the cost or limit the availability of the Company's products and the components and rare earths and other raw materials that go into them.
The Company uses some custom components that are not commonly used by its competitors, and new products introduced by the Company often utilize custom components available from only one source. When a component or product uses new technologies, initial capacity constraints may exist until the suppliers' yields have matured or their manufacturing capacities have increased. The Company has entered into agreements for the supply of many components; however, the Company may not be able to extend or renew agreements for the supply of components on similar terms, or at all, and may not be successful in obtaining sufficient quantities from its suppliers or in a timely manner, or in identifying and obtaining sufficient quantities from an alternative source. In addition, component suppliers may fail, be subject to consolidation within a particular industry, or decide to concentrate on the production of common components instead of components customized to meet the Company's requirements, further limiting the Company's ability to obtain sufficient quantities of components on commercially reasonable terms, or at all.
Research and Development
Because the industries in which the Company competes are characterized by rapid technological advances, the Company's ability to compete successfully depends heavily upon its ability to ensure a continual and timely flow of competitive products, services and technologies to the marketplace. The Company continues to develop new technologies to enhance existing products and services, and to expand the range of its offerings through research and development ("R&D"), licensing of intellectual property and acquisition of third-party businesses and technology.
Intellectual Property
The Company currently holds a broad collection of intellectual property rights relating to certain aspects of its hardware, software and services. This includes patents, designs, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets and other forms of intellectual property rights in the U.S. and various foreign countries. Although the Company believes the ownership of such intellectual property rights is an important factor in differentiating its business and that its success does depend in part on such ownership, the Company relies primarily on the innovative skills, technical competence and marketing abilities of its personnel.
The Company regularly files patent, design, copyright and trademark applications to protect innovations arising from its hardware, software and service research, development, design and marketing, and is currently pursuing thousands of applications around the world. Over time, the Company has accumulated a large portfolio of issued and registered intellectual property rights around the world. No single intellectual property right is solely responsible for protecting the Company's products and services. The Company believes the duration of its intellectual property rights is adequate relative to the expected lives of its products and services.
Apple Inc. | 2025 Form 10-K | 3
In addition to Company-owned intellectual property, many of the Company's products and services include technology or intellectual property that must be licensed from third parties. It may be necessary in the future to seek or renew licenses relating to various aspects of the Company's products, processes and services. While the Company has generally been able to obtain such licenses on commercially reasonable terms in the past, there is no guarantee that such licenses could be obtained in the future on reasonable terms or at all.
Business Seasonality and Product Introductions
The Company has historically experienced higher net sales in its first quarter compared to other quarters in its fiscal year due in part to seasonal holiday demand. Additionally, new product and service introductions can significantly impact net sales, cost of sales and operating expenses. The timing of product introductions can also impact the Company's net sales to its indirect distribution channels as these channels are filled with new inventory following a product launch, and channel inventory of an older product often declines as the launch of a newer product approaches. Net sales can also be affected when consumers and distributors anticipate a product introduction.
Human Capital
The Company believes that its people play an important role in its success, and strives to attract, develop and retain the best talent. The Company works to create a culture of collaboration, one where people with a broad range of backgrounds and perspectives can come together to innovate and do the best work of their lives. The Company is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and to providing a workplace free of harassment or discrimination. As of September 27, 2025, the Company had approximately 166,000 full-time equivalent employees.
The Company believes that compensation should be competitive and equitable, and offers discretionary cash and equity awards to enable employees to share in the Company's success. The Company recognizes its people are most likely to thrive when they have the resources to meet their needs and the time and support to succeed in their professional and personal lives. In support of this, the Company offers a wide variety of benefits for employees around the world, including health, wellness and time away.
The Company invests in resources to help its people develop and achieve their career goals. The Company offers programs through Apple University on leadership, management and influence, as well as Apple culture and values. Team members can also take advantage of online classes for business, technical and personal development.
The Company believes that open and honest communication among team members, managers and leaders helps create an open, collaborative work environment where everyone can contribute, grow and succeed. Team members are encouraged to come to their managers with questions, feedback or concerns, and the Company conducts surveys that gauge employee sentiment in areas like career development, manager performance and inclusion.
The Company is committed to the safety and security of its team members everywhere it operates. The Company supports employees with general safety, security and crisis management training, and by putting specific programs in place for those working in potentially high-hazard environments.
Available Information
The Company's Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K, and amendments to reports filed pursuant to Sections 13(a) and 15(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended ("Exchange Act"), are filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC"). Such reports and other information filed by the Company with the SEC are available free of charge at investor.apple.com/investor-relations/sec-filings/default.aspx when such reports are available on the SEC's website. The Company periodically provides certain information for investors on its corporate website, www.apple.com, and its investor relations website, investor.apple.com. This includes press releases and other information about financial performance, information on corporate governance, and details related to the Company's annual meeting of shareholders. The information contained on the websites referenced in this Form 10-K is not incorporated by reference into this filing. Further, the Company's references to website URLs are intended to be inactive textual references only.

**Quantitative Report: AAPL (as of 2026-04-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** Strong recent performance. Latest Q1 (2025-12-27) revenue was $143.76B, with QoQ growth of +40.3% and YoY growth of +15.7%. TTM revenue is $435.62B. EPS diluted followed a similar pattern, with Q1 at $2.84 (QoQ +54.3%, YoY +18.3%). TTM net income is $117.78B.
*   **Margins:** Margins are robust and expanding. Latest Q1 gross margin was 48.2% ($69.23B / $143.76B), operating margin was 35.4% ($50.85B / $143.76B), and net margin was 29.3% ($42.10B / $143.76B). The supplied valuation data shows a trailing net margin of 0.27 (27%).
*   **Cash Flow:** Not available. All supplied cash flow statements are empty.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Equity has grown significantly, from $56.95B (2024-09-28) to $88.19B (2025-12-27). Total debt has decreased from $96.66B to $88.50B over the same period. The debt-to-equity ratio is 1.004.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** High. Net income growth is supported by expanding margins. The latest Q1 net margin of 29.3% is above the TTM average implied by the 0.27 (27%) net margin figure. The trajectory shows consistent profitability across all reported quarters.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Moderate. The debt-to-equity ratio of 1.004 indicates debt roughly equals equity. While debt is declining and equity is growing, the absolute debt level of $88.50B remains substantial. Total assets of $379.30B provide a base, but the equity cushion is $88.19B.

**3. Valuation vs. Company History**
*   **P/E Diluted:** The latest P/E is 31.914. This is at the lower end of its 365-day window range of 27.2 to 40.9. The sparse history shows the P/E was as high as 37.542 (2025-10-15) and as low as 30.778 (2025-05-05) within the past year.
*   **P/B Ratio:** The latest P/B is 42.621. This is at the very bottom of its 365-day window range of 39.1 to 61.6, having declined from a high of 61.6.
*   **Profitability Ratios:** ROE is 1.335 (133.5%), and net margin is 0.27 (27%). Both are within their recent historical ranges (ROE: 1.3-1.5, net margin: 0.2-0.3).

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Price Level:** The latest close is $253.79. The 365-day trading range is $169.21 to $288.62.
*   **Quarterly Return:** The price declined -7.3% over the last ~63 trading days (from $273.76 on 2025-12-29 to $253.79).
*   **Annual Return:** The price increased +16.5% over the last ~252 trading days (from $217.90 on 2025-03-28 to $253.79).
*   **Fundamental vs. Price Path:** The stock's recent quarterly price decline (-7.3%) occurred alongside strong reported Q1 2025 financial results (revenue +15.7% YoY, EPS +18.3% YoY). The annual price gain (+16.5%) is broadly in line with the TTM earnings growth trajectory.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Upside:** 1) **Earnings Growth & Margin Expansion:** TTM net income of $117.78B and a latest net margin of 29.3% demonstrate strong profitability. 2) **Valuation Compression:** The P/E of 31.914 and P/B of 42.621 are near the bottom of their recent ranges, potentially reflecting lower expectations. 3) **Balance Sheet Improvement:** Equity growth to $88.19B and debt reduction to $88.50B are positive trends.
*   **Downside:** 1) **High Absolute Valuation:** A P/E of ~32 and P/B of ~43 remain elevated multiples. 2) **Leverage:** Debt-to-equity of 1.004 indicates significant financial leverage. 3) **Missing Cash Flow Data:** Inability to assess free cash flow generation is a material evidence gap.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The fundamental trajectory is strong, marked by double-digit revenue and earnings growth and expanding margins. The balance sheet is improving but remains leveraged. Valuation has compressed from recent highs, with the P/E and P/B ratios at the lower end of their one-year ranges. The recent quarterly price decline contrasts with the strong reported quarter, potentially creating a more favorable entry point relative to recent history. However, the high absolute multiples and lack of cash flow data represent quantitative risks. The risk/reward appears moderately favorable, contingent on the durability of the earnings growth trajectory and the assumption that cash flow generation is consistent with reported earnings.
AAPL.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: Apple Inc. (AAPL)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
Based on the 2025 Form 10-K (filing_date=2025-10-31), Apple designs, manufactures, and markets a broad portfolio of hardware products (iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables) and sells a variety of related services (Advertising, AppleCare, Cloud, Digital Content, Payment Services). The company operates in highly competitive, global markets segmented geographically. Its success depends on continuous innovation, an integrated hardware-software-services ecosystem, and managing a complex supply chain that includes sole- or limited-source components. Apple holds significant intellectual property and employs approximately 166,000 people.

**2. Recent Narrative Developments (PIT: 2026-02-10 to 2026-04-01)**
*   **Investor Sentiment & Valuation (2026-03-31):** Warren Buffett stated he would buy "a whole lot" more Apple shares if the price declined, expressing continued confidence in Tim Cook's leadership and the products. Conversely, the market narrative highlights Apple as part of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks that have been under pressure, with a Vanguard Value ETF outperforming indices, suggesting a rotation away from growth.
*   **AI Strategy (2026-03-31):** Apple is reported to be taking a different approach to AI by partnering with Google's Gemini for its new Siri rather than building its own large language model. The rationale cited is leveraging its installed base of 2.5 billion devices and hardware focus.
*   **Market & Macroeconomic Context (2026-03-31):** Global markets show fragility, with a risk-off rotation into defensive sectors noted. However, a market rally occurred linked to easing geopolitical tensions, which benefited mega-cap tech.
*   **Regulatory & Legal (Multiple Dates):** Apple faces a major U.S. antitrust lawsuit (reported 2026-02-10, 2026-02-25, 2026-03-30) alleging it uses hardware and software limitations to maintain a monopoly with the iPhone and iOS. The company argues the suit threatens its design principles. Additionally, Apple Pay is involved in a $4 billion legal battle over stolen tech claims (2026-02-13), and it faces ongoing global regulatory pressure, including the EU's Digital Markets Act (2026-02-25).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Regulatory & Legal Overhang:** The active U.S. antitrust lawsuit and coordinated global regulatory assault represent a significant risk, potentially forcing changes to its integrated business model or resulting in substantial fines.
    *   **Supply Chain Vulnerability:** The 10-K explicitly notes reliance on single or limited sources for some custom components, exposing it to shortages, price fluctuations, and trade restrictions.
    *   **Competitive Intensity:** The company operates in markets characterized by aggressive price competition, rapid imitation, and low-cost competitors, which can pressure margins and market share.
    *   **AI Strategy Execution:** Its partnership-heavy approach to AI, rather than a fully in-house model, could be perceived as a lack of control and may leave it dependent on third-party innovation.

*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Entrenched Ecosystem & Brand Loyalty:** The massive installed device base and integrated ecosystem create high switching costs and recurring service revenue, providing a durable competitive moat.
    *   **Investor Confidence:** The reiterated backing from a major long-term investor like Warren Buffett provides a qualitative support for sentiment.
    *   **Scale and Diversification:** Its broad product and services portfolio and global geographic segments provide diversification against single-product or regional shocks.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral with a Cautious Tilt.**

The core business franchise, as detailed in the 10-K, remains a world-class ecosystem with high brand loyalty and a powerful services flywheel. However, the recent narrative (as of 2026-03-31) is dominated by material headwinds, most prominently the serious U.S. antitrust threat and broader regulatory pressures that directly challenge its operating model. While strategic moves like the Google Gemini partnership and continued support from cornerstone investors are positive offsets, they are overshadowed by the magnitude of the unresolved legal/regulatory risk and a market environment showing skepticism toward high-growth tech. The evidence points to a period of significant uncertainty for the company's strategic direction.

 do business. In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree. We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and privacy and data protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations. We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance. We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2026 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
. Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage. Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business. We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategy relating to talent, including equality and inclusion, as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices. The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2025, the Company had approximately 109,000 employees, an increase of 2% versus the prior year due to business growth. The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures. 49% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 28% of our employees are located in the United States. 42% of our global employees are women and 32% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining the broadest pool of talent available, both from universities and the broader market. We recruit from universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent. We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities. Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Equality and Inclusion
As a global consumer products company, P&G serves consumers around the world with operations in approximately 70 countries. We believe that it is good for business that our workforce is drawn from the best available talent from communities worldwide, with insights about, connectivity to and understanding of all our consumers. Further, a foundational component of P&G's strategy and success has been to foster an inclusive work environment, in which we develop and advance the very best capabilities that all our people have to offer. Globally, we aspire to reflect the diversity of the consumers we serve in the communities where we operate and are committed to creating a superior employee experience for all. Our aspiration is founded on our longstanding values and principles for equal opportunity and compliance with the law.
Compensation and Benefits
Market-competitive compensation and reward programs are critical elements of our employee value equation to attract and retain the best talent. Our total rewards programs are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs. We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance, in line with local country practices, with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
. Environmental sustainability is integrated into our business strategy. We are focused on designing and manufacturing irresistibly superior products that are more sustainable. We aim to reduce our own environmental footprint and
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
enable our consumers to reduce their footprint without compromising on the performance of the products they use. We develop and license technologies that can be used across industries to improve environmental sustainability at a broader scale.
Combined, this approach intends to positively impact the total environmental impact of the Company while driving market growth and value creation.
In 2021, the Company announced a 2040 net zero ambition. Our Climate Transition Action Plan outlines the Company's ongoing efforts toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions across scopes 1 and 2 and elements of scope 3. The Company has also declared ambitions towards purchasing renewable electricity for our operations, reducing intensity of virgin petroleum-based plastic in packaging, designing more consumer packaging to be recyclable or reusable, responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities, improving efficiency of water usage in our operations and driving a global portfolio of water restoration projects in key water basins. While we have met or exceeded some of the goals and are making significant progress against others, scalable infrastructure and cost-effective solutions are not yet available to us to fully deliver against some of our stated ambitions.
Sustainability related disclosures included in this Annual Report, our Proxy Statement and our sustainability reports are informed by standards and guidelines such as the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD). The "materiality" thresholds in those standards and guidelines may differ from the concept of "materiality" for purposes of the federal securities laws and disclosures required by the Commission's rules in this Annual Report. References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.

**Quantitative Equity Report: PG**  
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01  

---

### 1. Financial Trajectory
**Revenue & Earnings:**  
- TTM Revenue (sum of last 4 quarters ending 2025-12-31): **86.25B**.  
- Latest quarter (2025-12-31): Revenue **22.21B**, Net Income **4.33B**, EPS Diluted **1.78**.  
- YoY Growth (latest quarter): Revenue **+1.5%**, EPS **-5.3%**, Net Income **-7.0%**.  
- QoQ Growth (latest quarter): Revenue **-0.8%**, EPS **-8.7%**, Net Income **-9.4%**.  

**Margins:**  
- Gross Margin (2025-12-31): **51.3%** (11.37B / 22.21B).  
- Operating Margin (2025-12-31): **23.8%** (5.37B / 22.21B).  
- Net Margin (2025-12-31): **18.6%** (4.33B / 22.21B).  

**Cash Flow & Balance Sheet:**  
- **Cash flow data is unavailable** for all periods.  
- Balance Sheet (2025-12-31): Total Assets **127.29B**, Total Equity **53.32B**, Total Liabilities **73.97B**.  
- Equity grew from **50.33B** (2024-03-31) to **53.32B** (+5.9% over 21 months).  

---

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience
**Earnings Quality:**  
- Net margin stable at **0.186** over the past year (range: 0.2–0.2).  
- ROE (latest): **29.7%** (range: 0.3–0.3 over past year).  
- Declining EPS growth (-5.3% YoY) amid modest revenue growth indicates margin pressure.  

**Balance-Sheet Resilience:**  
- Total Equity/Total Assets ratio: **41.9%** (53.32B / 127.29B).  
- Liabilities-to-Equity ratio: **1.39x** (73.97B / 53.32B).  
- No cash flow data to assess debt servicing or free cash flow coverage.  

---

### 3. Valuation vs. History
**Valuation Ratios (Latest 2026-03-31):**  
- P/E Diluted: **22.7** (range over past year: 21.7–27.2).  
- P/B: **6.73** (range over past year: 6.4–8.2).  
- Earnings Yield: **4.4%**.  

**Context:**  
- Current P/E of **22.7** is near the low end of its 1-year range (21.7–27.2).  
- P/B of **6.73** is at the low end of its range (6.4–8.2).  

---

### 4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals
**Price Performance:**  
- Latest Close (2026-03-31): **$144.44**.  
- 52-Week Range: **$137.62** (low) to **$174.80** (high).  
- Quarterly Return (63d): **-0.1%**.  
- Annual Return (252d): **-14.0%**.  

**Fundamental Context:**  
- Price decline of **-14.0%** over the past year aligns with negative EPS growth (-5.3% YoY) and flat-to-declining margins.  
- Current price is **-17.4%** below the 52-week high of **$174.80**.  

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside
**Upside:**  
- Valuation mean-reversion potential: P/E could revert toward **25–27** (historical range), implying **+10–19%** price upside if earnings stabilize.  
- Consistent margins (gross margin **51.3%**, net margin **18.6%**) and high ROE (**29.7%**) support earnings durability.  

**Downside:**  
- Continued EPS erosion (-5.3% YoY, -8.7% QoQ) may compress multiples further.  
- Annual price return of **-14.0%** suggests momentum headwinds if fundamentals do not improve.  
- Balance-sheet leverage (Liabilities/Equity **1.39x**) limits flexibility amid rising costs.  

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion
**Quantitative Summary:**  
- **Risk:** Earnings trajectory is weakening (EPS down -5.3% YoY), and price is in a negative trend (-14.0% YoY). Absent cash flow data obscures financial flexibility.  
- **Reward:** Valuation is near historical lows (P/E **22.7** vs. range 21.7–27.2; P/B **6.73** vs. 6.4–8.2). Margin stability and high ROE (**29.7%**) provide a fundamental floor.  

**Conclusion:**  
The stock reflects weak earnings momentum in both price and multiples, but valuation is approaching levels where historical mean reversion could occur. Upside depends on earnings stabilization; downside risks persist if margin compression continues.  

---  
**Data Gaps:** Free cash flow and cash flow statement data unavailable. All claims derived strictly from supplied evidence.
PG.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Procter & Gamble (PG)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Based on the 10-K filing (2025-08-04), Procter & Gamble is a world-leading multinational consumer goods company. Its business model focuses on creating, manufacturing, marketing, and distributing a diversified portfolio of daily-use branded products across approximately 180 countries. The company emphasizes balanced top- and bottom-line growth through innovation, marketing, and productivity improvements. It competes in highly competitive markets against both global branded competitors and private-label brands, often holding leadership positions. Key customers include mass merchandisers, e-commerce, and grocery stores, with Walmart Inc. representing approximately 16% of total sales.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Dividend & Defensive Positioning (2026-03-14, 2026-03-15, 2026-03-20, 2026-03-25, 2026-03-26):** Multiple articles consistently highlight P&G as a "Dividend King" with 69 consecutive years of dividend increases, recommending it as a stable, defensive investment during market volatility and rising oil prices.
*   **Cost & Supply Chain Pressures (2026-03-22, 2026-03-26):** News reports indicate that rising oil prices and a potential prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz could increase ingredient, packaging, and logistics costs for consumer staples companies like P&G, potentially pressuring margins.
*   **Innovation & Growth Catalysts (2026-02-17, 2026-02-19, 2026-02-27, 2026-02-28):** Web context from February 2026 notes analyst focus on P&G's brand strength and innovation (e.g., extending its anti-dandruff franchise, packaging-free laundry products) as core growth and margin catalysts. A February 28, 2026, analysis states that Q2 FY26 results showed the business (excluding a one-time Family Care decline) grew nearly 3% outside the U.S.
*   **Market Tailwinds (2026-03-26):** A market report projects the global dry shampoo market to grow at a 4.3% CAGR through 2030, a category relevant to P&G's hair care portfolio.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Legal & Regulatory (2026-03-11, 2026-03-26, 2026-03-29):** The company faces a class action lawsuit investigation (2026-03-11), lawsuits related to Old Spice product contamination (2026-03-26), and allegations of melatonin supplement dosage issues in Vicks products (2026-03-29). The 10-K (2025-08-04) confirms exposure to extensive global regulations (FDA, EPA, etc.).
    *   **Input Cost Inflation (2026-03-22, 2026-03-26):** Geopolitical events driving higher oil prices directly threaten costs for packaging and transportation.
    *   **Intense Competition (10-K, 2025-08-04):** The 10-K explicitly states markets are "highly competitive" from global, local, and private-label brands.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Brand Strength & Innovation (10-K, 2025-08-04; Web Context 2026-02-17, 2026-02-27):** The company's strategy is built on "irresistible superiority" and strong brand equity, which is cited as a key differentiator and growth driver.
    *   **Defensive Business Model (Multiple news articles, 2026-03-14 to 2026-03-26):** The essential nature of its products and consistent dividend history provide a defensive characteristic, supporting investor demand during economic uncertainty.
    *   **Global Scale & Distribution (10-K, 2025-08-04):** Operations in ~180 countries and a diversified customer base mitigate single-market or single-customer risk.

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The evidence points to a resilient, world-class consumer staples franchise with a durable competitive moat built on brand strength and innovation. Recent narrative (as of early 2026) highlights its status as a defensive "Dividend King" and identifies specific growth catalysts in innovation and international markets. While material risks exist—particularly from input cost inflation, ongoing litigation, and intense competition—the company's scale, brand portfolio, and strategic focus on superiority provide significant offsets. The overall qualitative picture, based on the supplied point-in-time evidence, is one of stability and incremental growth potential.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: PG is a high-quality defensive franchise trading near historical valuation lows (P/E 22.7 vs. 1-year range 21.7–27.2), but weakening earnings momentum (EPS -5.3% YoY, -8.7% QoQ) and rising input cost headwinds from oil prices limit near-term upside. The risk/reward is balanced: valuation mean-reversion offers modest upside if earnings stabilize, but continued margin compression and negative price momentum (-14.0% YoY) argue against aggressive positioning.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG is a high-quality defensive franchise trading near historical valuation lows (P/E 22.7 vs. 1-year range 21.7–27.2), but weakening earnings momentum (EPS -5.3% YoY, -8.7% QoQ) and rising input cost headwinds from oil prices limit near-term upside. The risk/reward is balanced: valuation mean-reversion offers modest upside if earnings stabilize, but continued margin compression and negative price momentum (-14.0% YoY) argue against aggressive positioning.
key_factors: Valuation near historical lows: P/E of 22.7 sits at the low end of its 1-year range (21.7–27.2) and P/B of 6.73 is at the low end of its range (6.4–8.2), offering mean-reversion potential of +10–19% if multiples revert toward 25–27.

Weakening earnings trajectory: EPS declined -5.3% YoY and -8.7% QoQ in the latest quarter (2025-12-31), with net income down -7.0% YoY, indicating margin pressure despite stable gross margins of 51.3%.

Input cost and geopolitical headwinds: Rising oil prices and potential Strait of Hormuz closure (noted 2026-03-22, 2026-03-26) threaten to increase ingredient, packaging, and logistics costs, pressuring the 23.8% operating margin.

Defensive franchise quality: 69 consecutive years of dividend increases, high ROE of 29.7%, and brand-driven innovation (anti-dandruff franchise extension, packaging-free laundry products) provide a fundamental floor and support the Hold rather than a more negative rating.

At NIKE, we consider the strength and effective management of our workforce to be essential to the ongoing success of our business. We believe that it is important to attract, develop and retain an engaged workforce with diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives at all levels of our business and that such a workforce fosters creativity and accelerates innovation.
CULTURE
Each employee shapes NIKE's culture through behaviors and practices. This starts with our Maxims, which represent our core values and, along with our Code of Conduct, feature the fundamental behaviors that help anchor, inform and guide us and apply to all employees. Our mission is to bring inspiration and innovation to every athlete in the world, which includes the belief that if you have a body, you are an athlete. We aim to do this by creating groundbreaking sport innovations, making our products more sustainably, building a creative global team with diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives, supporting the well-being of our employees and making a positive impact in communities where we live and work. Our mission is aligned with our deep commitment to maintaining an environment where all NIKE employees have the opportunity to reach their full potential, to connect to our brands and to shape our workplace culture. We believe providing for growth and retention of our employees is essential in fostering such a culture and are dedicated to providing access to training programs and career development opportunities, including trainings on NIKE's values, history and business, trainings on developing leadership skills at all levels, tools and resources for managers and qualified tuition reimbursement opportunities.
We are committed to having an inclusive and diverse team and culture, and accessible workplace. We achieve this through recruitment, development and retention of qualified talent with diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives through traditional channels, initiatives and partnerships, including those that serve colleges and universities. Additionally, we provide access to education so that all NIKE employees and leaders have the cultural knowledge and understanding to lead inclusively and build diverse and inclusive teams. We also have employee resource groups, collectively known as NikeUNITED, that promote NIKE cultural awareness and are open to all.
In empowering our employees to help shape our culture, we source employee feedback through a variety of survey tools: our annual Engagement Survey program, corporate pulse surveys and listening sessions. These tools provide employees throughout the globe an opportunity to provide confidential feedback on key areas known to drive employee engagement, including their satisfaction with their managers, their work and the Company generally. These tools also measure our employees' connection to NIKE's culture. NIKE also provides multiple points of contact for employees to speak up if they experience something that does not align with our values or otherwise violates our workplace policies, even if they are uncertain what they observed or heard is a violation of company policy.
As part of our commitment to making a positive impact on our communities, we maintain a goal of investing 2% of our prior fiscal year's pre-tax income into global communities. The focus of this investment continues to be inspiring youth to be active through play and sport as well as uniting and inspiring communities. Through our investments we bring the power of sport into our communities, with the goal of making play and sport more accessible. Our community investments are an important part of our culture, and we support employees in giving back to community organizations through volunteering and donations, which are matched by the NIKE Foundation where eligible.
EMPLOYEE BASE
As of May 31, 2025, we had approximately 77,800 employees worldwide, including retail and part-time employees. We also utilize independent contractors and temporary personnel to supplement our workforce.
Most of our employees are not represented by unions, except for certain employees in the EMEA and APLA geographies who are members of and/or are represented by trade unions, as allowed or required by local law and/or collective bargaining agreements. Also, in some countries outside of the United States, local laws require employee representation by works councils (which may be entitled to information and consultation on certain subsidiary decisions) or by organizations similar to a union. In certain European countries, we are required by local law to enter into, and/or comply with, industry-wide or national collective bargaining agreements. NIKE has never experienced a material interruption of operations due to labor disagreements.
COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS
NIKE's total rewards are intended to be competitive and equitable, meet the varied needs of our global teammates and reinforce our values. We are committed to providing comprehensive, competitive and equitable pay and benefits to our employees, and we have invested, and aim to continue to invest, in our employees through growth and development and holistic well-being initiatives. Our initiatives in this area include:
2025 FORM 10-K       6

- We are committed to competitive pay, pay equity and to reviewing our pay and promotion practices annually.
- We have an annual company bonus plan and a retail-focused bonus plan applicable to all eligible employees. Both programs are focused on rewarding employees for company performance, which we believe reinforces our culture and rewards behaviors that support collaboration and teamwork.
- We provide comprehensive family care benefits in the U.S. and globally where practicable, including family planning coverage, backup care and child/elder care assistance as well as an income-based childcare subsidy for eligible employees.
- Our Military Leave benefit provides up to 12 weeks of paid time off every 12 months.
- We offer free access to our sport centers at our World Headquarters for our full-time employees and North America store employees.
- We provide employees free access to mindfulness and meditation resources, as well as live classes through our sport centers.
- We provide all employees and their families globally with free and confidential visits with a mental health counselor through a third-party provider and our global Employee Assistance Program (EAP).
- We provide support to our employees in a variety of ways during times of crisis, including pay continuity under certain circumstances, and our natural disaster assistance program.
- We provide a hybrid work approach for the majority of employees, as well as a Four Week Flex program, which provides employees an opportunity to work remotely for up to four weeks per year.
