THE GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES
Human Capital Management
Our people are our greatest asset. We believe that a major strength and principal reason for our success is the quality, dedication, determination and collaboration of our people, which enables us to serve our clients, generate long-term value for our shareholders and contribute to the broader community.
Our Workforce
Our goal is to attract, retain, and promote an exceptionally skilled workforce. We invest heavily in developing and supporting our people throughout their careers, and we strive to maintain a work environment that fosters professionalism, excellence, high standards of business ethics, teamwork and cooperation among our employees worldwide. We believe that the diversity of our workforce, including diversity of perspectives, enhances our performance-based culture and is critical to our commercial success. We remain focused on the importance of attracting and retaining diverse exceptional talent. We will continue to develop programs consistent with our fundamental commitment to inclusive, merit-based promotion and in compliance with the law.
Talent Development and Retention
We seek to help our people achieve their full potential by investing in them and supporting a culture of continuous development. Our goals are to maximize individual capabilities, increase commercial effectiveness and innovation, reinforce our culture, expand professional opportunities, and help our people contribute positively to their communities. As of December 2025, the average tenure of the members of our Management Committee was approximately 23 years, and that of all of our employees was approximately 6 years. In addition, nearly 45% of our partners were campus hires.
Instilling our culture in all employees is a continuous process, in which training plays an important part. We offer our employees the opportunity to participate in ongoing educational offerings and periodic seminars facilitated by our Talent Development team. To accelerate their integration into the firm and our culture, new hires have the opportunity to receive training as soon as they start working via orientation programs that emphasize culture and networking, and nearly all employees participate in at least one training event each year. For our more senior employees, we provide guidance and training on how to manage people and projects effectively, exhibit strong leadership and exemplify our culture. We are also focused on developing a high performing, diverse leadership pipeline and career planning for our next generation of leaders. We maintain a variety of programs aimed at employees' professional growth and leadership development. For example, we are focused on ensuring that vice presidents and managing directors have the necessary coaching, sponsorship and advocacy to support their career trajectories and strengthen their leadership platforms. Many other career development initiatives are aimed at fostering talent at the analyst and associate levels. We also work closely with our leadership teams to promote diversity and inclusion. Our global and regional Inclusion Networks and Interest Forums are open to all of our professionals to promote and advance these goals.
Enhancing our people's experience of internal mobility is a key focus, as we believe that this will inspire employees, help retain top talent and create diverse experiences to build future leaders.
Another important part of instilling our culture is our employee performance review process. Employees are reviewed by supervisors, co-workers and employees whom they supervise in a 360-degree review process that is integral to our team approach and includes an evaluation of an employee's performance. Our approach to evaluating employee performance centers on providing robust, timely and actionable feedback that facilitates professional development. We have directed our managers, as leaders at the firm, to take an active coaching role with their teams. We also engage in "Three Conversations at GS" through which managers establish goals with their team members at the start of the year, check in mid-year on progress and then close out the year with a conversation on performance against goals.

GS.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Assessment: Goldman Sachs (GS) as of 2026-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** Q4 2025 revenue was $13.45B, down 11.4% QoQ and 3.0% YoY. TTM revenue is $58.28B.
- **Earnings:** Q4 2025 EPS diluted was $13.99, up 14.2% QoQ and 17.6% YoY. TTM net income is $17.18B.
- **Margins:** Latest net margin is 29.5% and operating margin is 37.5% (as of 2026-03-31). Margins have improved from net margin of 26.7% in April 2025.
- **Cash Flow:** No cash flow data available.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total equity was $124.97B in Q4 2025, up from $118.55B in Q1 2024. Leverage (assets/equity) is 14.5 (total assets $1809.32B).

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Without cash flow data, cash conversion cannot be assessed. EPS growth (17.6% YoY) outpaced revenue decline (-3.0% YoY), suggesting improved profitability but potential reliance on non-operating factors.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Equity has grown steadily (from $118.55B to $124.97B over 8 quarters), but leverage is high (equity ratio of 6.9%). No liquidity metrics are available.

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**
- **Latest Valuation:** P/E diluted is 16.485, P/B is 2.15, earnings yield is 6.1% (as of 2026-03-31).
- **Historical Range:** Over the past year, P/E ranged from 11.4 to 18.4, and P/B from 1.3 to 2.5. Current P/E is near the upper end (84th percentile), and P/B is also elevated.
- **Trend:** Valuation multiples have expanded: P/E increased from 13.529 (April 2025) to 16.485, and P/B from 1.5 to 2.15, alongside improving ROE from 11.7% to 13.7%.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
- **Price:** Close of $845.99 on 2026-03-31, with a 52-week range of $439.38 to $984.70.
- **Returns:** Quarterly return is -5.2% (from $892.18), annual return is +55.8% (from $543.12).
- **Fundamental vs. Price Path:** The stock has outperformed fundamentals: annual revenue growth is -3.0%, while price rose 55.8%, driven by earnings growth and multiple expansion.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside:** Continued earnings growth (EPS grew 17.6% YoY) and margin expansion (net margin from 26.7% to 29.5%) could support further price appreciation if multiples hold or expand.
- **Downside:** Valuation contraction to the lower end of the historical P/E range (11.4) would imply a price of ~$593 (using TTM EPS of ~$52), a 30% decline. Negative revenue growth could pressure earnings if it persists.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The stock trades at elevated multiples (P/E 16.485, near the top of its 11.4–18.4 range) despite negative revenue growth (-3.0% YoY). Earnings growth is strong but may be unsustainable without revenue improvement. Balance sheet leverage is high but typical. The risk is multiple contraction given stretched valuations; reward depends on continued earnings beats. Quantitatively, downside risk from valuation reversion appears significant relative to upside potential, implying unfavorable risk/reward.
GS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Goldman Sachs (GS)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**
**Evidence Window: 2026-02-10 to 2026-03-31**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K filed 2026-02-25)
Goldman Sachs is a leading global financial institution operating through three segments: **Global Banking & Markets (GBM)**, **Asset & Wealth Management (AWM)**, and **Platform Solutions**. The firm's purpose is to advance sustainable economic growth, with a strategic goal under "One Goldman Sachs" to deliver services more comprehensively.
*   **GBM** is the core institutional business, focused on advisory/underwriting fees and FICC/Equities intermediation/financing. It has recently incorporated transaction banking and other financing activities to better reflect the integrated institutional platform.
*   **AWM** provides investment and wealth services across diverse strategies, generating fees from management, incentives, and lending. It is transitioning from direct balance-sheet investments to a third-party funds model.
*   **Platform Solutions** is being strategically narrowed, with its activities now substantially limited to the Apple Card program (which is being transitioned away as of December 2025) and exited businesses. This represents a deliberate retreat from broad consumer finance.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)
*   **Corporate Action (2026-02-25):** Goldman completed the sale of the GM credit card program and entered an agreement to transition the Apple Card program, expected to take ~24 months, substantially concluding its consumer focus narrowing.
*   **Market Activity & Outlook (2026-03-31, 2026-03-26):** The firm's stock rose 4.95% on March 31, attributed to favorable regulatory developments and a projected surge in M&A. This is consistent with earlier narratives citing Goldman's "dominance in M&A" and a "resurgence in investment banking" as key growth catalysts.
*   **Strategy (2026-02-26, 2026-03-03):** Goldman Sachs Research published an optimistic S&P 500 forecast for 2026. The firm also identified financials, industrials, and energy as sectors absorbing capital, while maintaining its view on mega-tech's potential revival.
*   **Competitive Position & Market Environment (2026-03-28, 2026-03-27, 2026-03-26):** Broader bank stocks were noted as potential bargains (financial sector P/E at 14). The financial sector showed stabilization signs after "significant losses in 2026," with major banks "holding steady."

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Litigation & Reputational Exposure:** A securities class action lawsuit (filed 2026-03-24) alleges Goldman failed to disclose a 39% surge in sales/marketing expenses as lead underwriter for the Navan IPO (Oct. 2025), with shares down 63%. Separately, a court ruled (2026-03-02) Goldman cannot escape a lawsuit over its role in the EngageSmart deal involving a hidden payment. Additional lawsuits allege age discrimination (2026-03-18). A separate class action (cited 2026-03-11) has been filed against GS securities purchasers.
*   **Regulatory & Compliance Precedent:** Past actions, including the 1MDB scandal settlement of $5.4 billion (referenced 2026-03-26), establish a history of significant regulatory fines.
*   **Macro & Geopolitical Headwinds:** The evidence cites recession warnings (2026-03-19) amid elevated valuations and geopolitical tensions (Iran conflict). Such conditions can depress capital markets activity and affect asset valuations in AWM.
*   **Consumer Business Execution:** While the Apple Card transition is a deliberate exit, it represents the unwinding of a large, high-profile partnership, involving execution risk over a 24-month period.

**Offsets:**
*   **Franchise Strength in Core Business:** GBM's leadership in advisory and underwriting, especially in large, complex M&A, is a repeatedly cited growth engine.
*   **Strategic Focus:** The deliberate narrowing of consumer exposure (via Apple Card and GM card exits) reduces operational complexity and risk in non-core areas, refocusing capital on higher-margin institutional and wealth activities.
*   **Favorable Sector Positioning:** The firm is highlighted as being in a sector perceived as a "bargain" within an otherwise expensive market, potentially attracting capital inflows.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral-Slight Negative.**

**Confirmed Evidence:** The franchise has a strong, repositioned institutional core (GBM) and is intentionally shedding volatile consumer business (Platform Solutions). Recent developments highlight a resurgence in investment banking activity, which is a direct tailwind.
**Confirmed Evidence/Inference Balance:** However, the firm faces a material headwind from active, specific securities litigation (Navan IPO, EngageSmart) and retains a legacy of significant regulatory penalties. The macro backdrop is uncertain, with explicit recession warnings in the evidence window. The positive narrative around M&A and sector valuation is somewhat offset by the company's specific legal risks and the broader market's "death cross" technical conditions noted on 2026-03-26. The net qualitative picture is one of a strong franchise pursuing a sound strategy, but currently navigating meaningful legal and environmental uncertainties.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Goldman Sachs trades at an elevated P/E of 16.5 (84th percentile of its 11.4–18.4 range) despite negative revenue growth of -3.0% YoY, creating a valuation that prices in significant earnings improvement. While the franchise is strong with GBM leadership and a favorable M&A resurgence narrative, multiple contraction risk from stretched valuations and active securities litigation offset the earnings momentum, resulting in a roughly balanced risk/reward.

ITEM 1.    BUSINESS
Introduction
The Sherwin-Williams Company, founded in 1866 and incorporated in Ohio in 1884, is engaged in the development, manufacture, distribution and sale of paint, coatings and related products to professional, industrial, commercial and retail customers primarily in North and South America with additional operations in the Caribbean region, Europe, Asia and Australia. Our principal executive offices are located at 1 Sherwin Way, Cleveland, Ohio 44113-2206, telephone (216) 566-2000. As used in this report, the terms "Sherwin-Williams," "Company," "we", "us" and "our" mean The Sherwin-Williams Company and its consolidated subsidiaries.
Available Information
We make available free of charge on or through our website our Annual Reports on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q, Current Reports on Form 8-K and amendments to these reports, as soon as reasonably practicable after we electronically file such material with, or furnish such material to, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
We also make available free of charge on our website our Corporate Governance Guidelines, our Director Independence Standards, our Code of Conduct and the charters of our Audit Committee, our Compensation and Management Development Committee and our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. You may access these documents on our Investor Relations website, investors.sherwin.com.
Basis of Reportable Segments
The Company reports its segment information in the same way that management internally organizes its business for assessing performance and making decisions regarding allocation of resources. The Company has three reportable operating segments: Paint Stores Group, Consumer Brands Group and Performance Coatings Group (individually, a Reportable Segment and collectively, the Reportable Segments). The Company reports all other business activities and immaterial operating segments that are not reportable in the Administrative function. For further information about the Reportable Segments, see Note 22 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.
Paint Stores Group
Paint Stores Group consisted of 4,853 company-operated specialty paint stores in the United States, Canada and the Caribbean region at December 31, 2025. Each store is engaged in servicing the needs of architectural and industrial paint contractors and do-it-yourself homeowners. These stores market and sell Sherwin-Williams® and other controlled brand architectural paint and coatings, protective and marine products, OEM product finishes and related products. The majority of these products are produced by manufac
…[truncated 14267 chars]…
nd advancing a pipeline of talent with a broad mix of skills, backgrounds and experiences. During 2025, we hired approximately 1,700 professionals through our management trainee program as part of our long-term growth initiatives.
We invest in our people by providing learning and employee networking opportunities to drive retention, development and engagement and help employees excel in their current and future roles. During 2025, our employees collectively completed thousands of hours of online and instructor-led courses across a broad range of categories, including leadership, professional skills, technical skills and compliance. We measure our progress toward creating a culture of excellence that empowers employees to learn, grow and achieve their aspirations by conducting periodic pulse surveys and a global engagement survey, which we conducted in 2025 and expect to conduct every other year. We are focused on using these survey results to drive continued progress with our efforts.
Occupational Health and Safety.
Providing safe and healthy working environments for our employees is a core value. We have a consistent focus on Environmental, Health and Safety excellence that promotes employee health and safety, process safety and occupational health, including evaluation and implementation of preventative measures to reduce workplace injuries and illness. We strive for incident-free workplaces and are continuously seeking to improve the programs that are in place to help keep our employees, customers and communities safe, including by regularly re-evaluating our global management systems, standards and performance measures.
Total Rewards.
We prioritize the fair and consistent treatment of our employees in relation to working conditions, wages, benefits, policies and procedures. The Company's policies and programs are designed to respond to the needs of our employees in a manner that provides a safe, professional, efficient and rewarding workplace. Our total rewards programs are designed to offer competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits and other programs to support employees' growth, both personally and professionally, and the needs and well-being of our employees worldwide.
Over the past few years, we have enhanced certain of the Company's benefits and practices to support the health and well-being of our employees. Our enhanced benefits have included paid sick leave, family leave and voluntary leave of absence policies and programs. In 2025, we introduced a new backup child and elder care benefit and increased the annual discount on medical plan contributions that employees can earn by participating in our well-being program. More recently, in 2026 we launched a new global well-being program and platform to give all employees access to engaging tools and resources that will help drive better health outcomes.
Regulatory Compliance
For further information regarding environmental-related matters, see Notes 1, 10 and 19 to the consolidated financial statements in Item 8.

- the infringement or loss of our intellectual property rights or the theft or unauthorized use of our trade secrets or other confidential business information;
- a weakening of global credit markets or changes to our credit ratings;
- our ability to generate cash to service our indebtedness;
- fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and changing monetary policies;
- our ability to comply with a variety of complex U.S. and non-U.S. laws, rules and regulations;
- increases in tax rates, or changes in tax laws or regulations;
- our ability to comply with numerous, complex and increasingly stringent domestic and foreign health, safety and environmental laws, regulations and requirements;
- our liability related to environmental investigation and remediation activities at some of our currently- and formerly-owned sites;
- the nature, cost, quantity and outcome of pending and future litigation, including lead pigment and lead-based paint litigation; and
- the other risk factors discussed in Item 1A of this Annual Report on Form 10-K and our other reports filed with the SEC.

SHW.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Equity Analysis: SHW**
**Date of Evidence:** 2026-04-01 (All data point-in-time prior)

**1. Financial Trajectory**
*   **Revenue & Earnings:** TTM revenue is $23.57B. The latest quarter (Q4 2025) shows revenue of $5.60B, a QoQ decline of -12.0% but a YoY increase of +5.6%. EPS followed a similar pattern, at $1.92 (QoQ: -42.7%, YoY: +1.1%). TTM net income is $2.57B.
*   **Margins:** Gross margin has compressed: 48.5% in Q4 2025 vs. 48.3% a year prior. The more significant shift is in operating margin, which fell to 11.4% in Q4 2025 from 16.7% in Q3 2025 and versus -3.1% in Q4 2024. The net margin for the latest reporting period is 10.9%.
*   **Cash Flow:** No quarterly or annual cash flow statement data is available for the past eight quarters.
*   **Balance Sheet:** Total assets are $25.90B, total equity is $4.60B, and total debt is $9.67B. Total equity has grown from $4.05B at 2024-12-31. The debt-to-equity ratio is 2.103.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
*   **Earnings Quality:** The large QoQ swing in operating income (from $1.06B in Q3 2025 to $639.00M in Q4 2025) indicates high operating leverage or volatility. Net income growth is decoupled from operating income trends (e.g., Q4 2024 had net income of $480.10M despite negative operating income), suggesting significant non-operating items or tax effects.
*   **Balance-Sheet Resilence:** The debt-to-equity ratio of 2.103 indicates high financial leverage. While total equity grew to $4.60B, it remains a small base relative to the $9.67B debt load and $25.90B total assets.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
*   **Current Valuation:** As of 2026-03-31, P/E (diluted) is 31.2x, P/B is 17.5x, and earnings yield is 3.2%.
*   **Historical Context (12-point series, Apr 2025 - Mar 2026):** The current P/E of 31.2x is at the lower end of the observed 1-year range (29.6x - 37.1x). P/B of 17.5x is also near its 1-year low of 16.5x. The earnings yield has compressed. Valuation multiples have declined alongside stable-to-declining net margins (from 0.116 to 0.109) and ROE (from 0.662 to 0.559).

**4. Price Level & Return Context**
*   **Price Action:** The closing price on 2026-03-31 is $320.55. Over the prior year (~252 trading days), it has declined -5.7% from $339.75. The quarterly return (from 2025-12-29) is -1.4%.
*   **Fundamental Path vs. Price:** The stock's annual price decline of -5.7% aligns with a period of flat YoY EPS growth (+1.1%) but declining profit margins and expanding valuation multiples' contraction. The price is near its 1-year low of $301.58.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
*   **Strongest Upside Potential:** A reversion in the P/E multiple to the middle of its recent range (~33.5x) combined with stabilize or growing EPS could drive price appreciation. Equity growth continues, improving book value.
*   **Strongest Downside Risk:** Further erosion of operating margins and the high debt load (Debt/Equity=2.103) create vulnerability in a higher-rate environment. The complete lack of cash flow data (0 of 8 quarters available) prevents assessment of cash conversion or debt serviceability, representing a significant analytical blind spot.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The data presents a mixed fundamental picture: revenue shows modest growth, but quarterly earnings and margins are volatile and declining, with compressed valuation multiples reflecting this. High leverage and absent cash flow data limit assessment of financial resilience. Price has underperformed slightly over the past year but operates near its low. The risk/reward appears balanced but skews to the downside given the trajectory of margins, high leverage, and the unquantifiable risk of poor cash generation. Measurable upside requires a stabilization and re-rating of currently depressed earnings power.

**Evidence Gaps:** Free cash flow data for all eight quarters is unavailable, preventing a cash-generation assessment.
SHW.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Research Report: Sherwin-Williams (SHW)**
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01
**Evidence Base:** News, web context, and 10-K narrative filed 2026-02-19.

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
Sherwin-Williams operates a vertically integrated, leading franchise in paints and coatings with three reportable segments. The **Paint Stores Group** is the core U.S. and Canadian distribution arm, with 4,853 company-operated stores (as of 2025-12-31) serving professional contractors and DIY customers. The **Consumer Brands Group** is the primary manufacturer for the company, also distributing branded and private-label products through retail channels. The **Performance Coatings Group** targets global industrial, automotive, and marine markets. The company possesses a portfolio of strong, recognized trademarks (e.g., Sherwin-Williams®, Valspar®, Krylon®, Minwax®). The franchise benefits from scale, integration, and a direct store model that provides technical expertise and service advantages.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Shareholder Returns & Capital Allocation:** Declared a quarterly dividend of $0.80 per share, marking the 47th consecutive year of dividend increases (2026-02-18).
*   **Earnings & Guidance:** The company beat Q4 2025 earnings estimates, reporting EPS of $2.23 vs. $2.16 expected, and provided 2026 diluted EPS guidance of $10.70–$11.10 (2026-03-17).
*   **Growth Initiatives:** Announced plans to open 80 to 100 new stores in 2026, leveraging industry leadership (2026-03-03). Investment in customer-facing initiatives during a period of competitor retrenchment is noted as a potential driver for share gains (2026-03-10).
*   **Industry & Macro Catalysts:** The CEO identified a 6% 30-year fixed mortgage rate as a "magic number" that could unlock pent-up housing demand, a key driver for the Paint Stores segment (2026-02-11). The global industrial coatings market is projected to grow at a 4.16% CAGR to $176.06 billion by 2035, driven by sustainability and innovation trends (2026-02-21).
*   **Analyst Sentiment:** Web context indicates analyst upgrades following the Q4 earnings beat (2026-03-17).

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Cyclical Demand & Macro Sensitivity:** The business is tied to the construction, industrial, and housing markets, which are sensitive to interest rates and economic cycles. The 10-K explicitly lists general economic conditions, inflation, and interest rates as key risk factors (2026-02-19).
*   **Risk: Raw Material Costs & Supply Chain:** Raw materials, often petrochemical-derived, represent a majority of cost of goods sold. The 10-K identifies shortages and cost increases as major risks (2026-02-19).
*   **Risk: Competitive Intensity:** The 10-K describes competition from local to international players across all segments, with factors including price, product quality, and service (2026-02-19).
*   **Risk: Regulatory & Environmental Compliance:** The 10-K notes numerous, stringent environmental laws and regulations as a risk, with the Consumer Brands Group incurring most related capital expenditures (2026-02-19). Broader environmental policy litigation (e.g., EPA-related lawsuits) could signal increasing regulatory focus in the sector (2026-02-18).
*   **Offset: Structural Competitive Advantages:** The company's direct store model, brand strength, scale, vertical integration, and investment during competitor weakness (2026-03-10) provide defenses against competitive and cyclical pressures.
*   **Offset: Proactive Shareholder Returns:** The long-term dividend growth streak signals management confidence and a commitment to returning capital (2026-02-18).

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.**
The narrative confirms a strong, well-branded franchise with a leading market position. Recent developments are constructive, including a solid earnings beat, continued dividend growth, and actionable growth plans (new store openings) that align with a period of competitor retrenchment. The identified macroeconomic "magic number" (6% mortgage rate) provides a clear, tangible catalyst tied to its core U.S. Paint Stores business. However, the assessment is tempered by the inherent cyclicality of its end markets and the persistent risks of input cost inflation and competition explicitly outlined in its SEC filing. The company appears to be navigating the current environment from a position of strategic strength.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Sherwin-Williams is a high-quality franchise with a leading market position and constructive recent developments (earnings beat, 47-year dividend streak, 80-100 new store openings in 2026), but the quantitative picture is mixed: flat YoY EPS growth (+1.1%), declining operating margins (11.4% vs. 16.7% prior quarter), high leverage (D/E=2.103), and a complete absence of cash flow data that prevents assessment of cash conversion. At 31.2x P/E near the low end of its 1-year range, valuation offers modest upside if margins stabilize, but the risk/reward is balanced given margin trajectory and unquantifiable cash generation risk.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Sherwin-Williams is a high-quality franchise with a leading market position and constructive recent developments (earnings beat, 47-year dividend streak, 80-100 new store openings in 2026), but the quantitative picture is mixed: flat YoY EPS growth (+1.1%), declining operating margins (11.4% vs. 16.7% prior quarter), high leverage (D/E=2.103), and a complete absence of cash flow data that prevents assessment of cash conversion. At 31.2x P/E near the low end of its 1-year range, valuation offers modest upside if margins stabilize, but the risk/reward is balanced given margin trajectory and unquantifiable cash generation risk.
key_factors: Revenue grew +5.6% YoY in Q4 2025 to $5.60B, but operating margin compressed sharply to 11.4% from 16.7% in Q3 2025, indicating high operating leverage and earnings volatility.

Debt-to-equity ratio of 2.103 ($9.67B debt vs. $4.60B equity) creates financial vulnerability, compounded by zero cash flow data across all eight quarters, leaving cash conversion and debt serviceability unassessable.
P/E of 31.2x is at the lower end of its 1-year range (29.6x-37.1x) and 2026 EPS guidance of $10.70-$11.10 implies ~10-14% growth, providing modest valuation support if earnings trajectory stabilizes.

Management is investing counter-cyclically with 80-100 new store openings in 2026 and a 47-year dividend increase streak, while the CEO identified a 6% mortgage rate as a catalyst for pent-up housing demand in the Paint Stores segment.

5
Table of Contents
Competitive Environment
Caterpillar products and services are sold worldwide into a variety of highly competitive markets.  In all markets, we compete on the basis of product performance, customer service, quality and price.  From time to time, the intensity of competition results in price discounting in a particular industry or region.  Such price discounting puts pressure on margins and can negatively impact operating profit. Outside the United States, certain competitors enjoy competitive advantages inherent to operating in their home countries or regions.
Raw Materials and Component Products
We source our raw materials and manufactured components from suppliers both domestically and internationally. These purchases include unformed materials and rough and finished parts.  Unformed materials include a variety of steel products, which are then cut or formed to shape and machined in our facilities. Rough parts include various sized steel and iron castings and forgings, which are machined to final specification levels inside our facilities. Finished parts are ready to assemble components, which are made either to Caterpillar specifications or to supplier developed specifications.  We machine and assemble some of the components used in our machines, engines and power generation units and to support our after-market dealer parts sales. We also purchase various goods and services used in production, logistics, offices and product development processes.  We maintain global strategic sourcing models to meet our global facilities' production needs while building long-term supplier relationships and leveraging enterprise spend.  We expect our suppliers to maintain, at all times, industry-leading levels of quality and the ability to timely deliver raw materials and component products for our machine and engine products. However, in some cases, increases in demand or supply chain disruptions have led to parts and components constraints across some products. We use a variety of agreements with suppliers to protect our intellectual property and processes to monitor and mitigate risks of the supply base causing a business disruption.  The risks monitored include supplier financial viability, the ability to increase or decrease production levels, business continuity, quality and delivery.
Patents and Trademarks
We own a number of patents and trademarks, which have been obtained over a period of years and relate to the products we manufacture and the services we provide. These patents and trademarks are generally considered beneficial to our business. We do not regard our business as being dependent upon any single patent or group of patents.
Order Backlog
The dollar amount of backlog believed to be firm was approximately $51.2 billion at December 31, 2025 and $30.0 billion at December 31, 2024. Compared with year-end 2024, the order backlog increased across the primary segments, with the largest increase in Power & Energy. Of the total backlog at December 31, 2025, approximately $19.3 billion was not expected to be filled in 2026.
Dealers and Distributors
We distribute our machines principally through a worldwide organization of dealers (dealer network), 41 located in the United States and 109 located outside the United States, serving 190 countries.  We sell reciprocating engines principally through the dealer network and to other manufacturers for use in products. We also sell some of the reciprocating engines manufactured by our subsidiary Perkins Engines Company Limited through its worldwide network of 86 distributors covering 183 countries.  We sell the FG Wilson branded electric power generation systems through its worldwide network of 108 distributors covering 159 countries.
Our dealers do not deal exclusively with our products; however, in most cases sales and servicing of our products are the dealers' principal business.  We sell some products, primarily turbines and locomotives, directly to end customers through sales forces employed by the company.  At times, these employees are assisted by independent sales representatives.
While the large majority of our worldwide dealers are independently owned and operated, we own and operate a dealership in Japan that covers approximately 80% of the Japanese market:  Nippon Caterpillar Division.  We are currently operating this Japanese dealer directly and we report its results in the All Other Segment.  There are also three independent dealers in the Southern Region of Japan.

Pursuant to these agreements, the company grants the dealer the right to purchase and sell its products and to service the products in a specified geographic service territory.  The company establishes prices to dealers after receiving input from dealers on transactional pricing in the marketplace.  The company also agrees to defend its intellectual property and to provide warranty and technical support to the dealer.  The agreement further grants the dealer a non-exclusive license to use the company's trademarks, service marks and brand names.  In some instances, a separate trademark agreement exists between the company and a dealer.

CAT.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative CAT Assessment (PIT: 2026-04-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue:** Consistent quarterly growth, from $14.25B (Q1'25) to $19.13B (Q4'25). TTM revenue is $67.59B. YoY Q4 revenue growth was +18.0%.
- **Earnings & Margins:** YoY Q4 eps_diluted declined -11.4% ($5.12 vs. $5.78). TTM net_income is $8.88B. Gross margin compressed YoY (30.4% to 30.5% is flat, but gross_profit fell -1.2% YoY). Operating margin deteriorated: Q4 operating_income of $2.66B on $19.13B revenue (13.9%) vs. prior Q4's $2.92B/$16.21B (18.0%).
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew to $98.58B. Leverage increased: total_liabilities-to-total_equity ratio rose from 3.5x (Q4'24) to 3.6x (Q4'25) = 77.27B / 21.32B.
- **Cash Flow:** Quarterly cash flow data is **unavailable**.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Negative signal. YoY eps_diluted down -11.4% and net_income down -13.9% despite top-line growth, indicating margin pressure. Gross_profit was down -1.2% YoY. TTM net_margin is 13.1%.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Equity growth ($17.64B to $21.32B over six quarters) is positive, but the rising liability-to-equity ratio (from 3.7x in Q1'25 to 3.6x in Q4'25) shows increasing financial leverage.

**3. Valuation vs. Supplied History**
- **Current Valuation (2026-03-31):** PE (diluted) = 37.664, P/B = 15.696, Net Margin = 13.1%, ROE = 41.7%.
- **Historical Context:** Over the past year, PE has expanded dramatically from a low of 12.4 to a high of 41.2. Current PE of 37.664 is near the top of its range. Net margin has contracted from 16.6% (April 2025) to 13.1%. ROE has fallen from 55.3% to 41.7%. The stock has re-rated significantly despite deteriorating margins and earnings growth.

**4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price Performance:** The stock price has surged. Quarterly return (last ~63 days) = +22.4%. Annual return (last ~252 days) = +114.9%.
- **Fundamental Comparison:** This massive price appreciation (+114.9%) contrasts with a YoY Q4 eps decline of -11.4% and sluggish annual net income growth (TTM: $8.88B). The price has outrun fundamental improvements in revenue.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Strongest Upside:** Current price momentum and historically elevated ROE (41.7%) and sales figure ($67.59B TTM).
- **Strongest Downside:** Extreme valuation (PE=37.7x) at a time of margin contraction (net margin 13.1% vs 16.6% prior year) and declining YoY earnings growth. The forward PE will expand further if the earnings decline continues.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The risk/reward is unfavorable. The valuation (PE 37.7x, P/B 15.7x) is at the top of its recent history, significantly distancing it from values observed at higher margin levels (16.6% net margin). This premium exists despite clear YoY earnings deceleration (-11.4% eps growth) and margin compression. The price has achieved a parabolic move (+114.9% annual) that is not supported by the recent fundamental trajectory of flat to declining profitability metrics. A reversion toward historical valuation multiples, absent a sharp earnings acceleration, presents substantial downside risk.
CAT.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01 | Evidence Cutoff: Before 2026-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (Confirmed via 10-K, filed 2026-02-13)**
Caterpillar is the world’s leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway engines, industrial gas turbines, and diesel-electric locomotives, with 2025 revenues of $67.589 billion. Its enterprise strategy is anchored by three growth pillars: Commercial Excellence, Advanced Technology Leader, and Transform How We Work. The business operates through four reportable segments:
*   **Construction Industries:** Supports infrastructure and building construction, competing globally with firms like Komatsu, Deere, and Volvo.
*   **Resource Industries:** Serves mining, heavy construction, and quarrying with equipment for surface and underground operations.
*   **Power & Energy:** Provides engines, generator sets, turbines, and services for oil and gas, power generation, marine, and rail.
*   **Financial Products:** Operates Cat Financial, offering retail/wholesale financing to customers and dealers, competing with commercial banks and other manufacturers’ finance arms.
A key competitive advantage is its global network of 150 dealers (41 in the U.S., 109 internationally). The firm holds a significant order backlog of $51.2 billion as of December 31, 2025.

**2. Dated Recent Developments (From Supplied News & Web Context)**
*   **AI/Data Center Infrastructure Catalyst (March 2026):** Multiple articles (2026-03-07, 2026-03-08, 2026-03-01, 2026-03-30) position CAT as a beneficiary of surging data center construction for AI, driving demand for construction equipment and backup power solutions. A specific partnership with Atlas Energy Solutions was announced on **2026-03-23**, involving an $840 million agreement to build 2 gigawatts of generation capacity by 2030.
*   **Reshoring & Infrastructure Tailwinds (February-March 2026):** News highlights CAT’s benefit from U.S. reshoring trends (2026-03-09 notes a 40%+ increase in U.S. manufacturing plant spending since 2020) and global infrastructure demand. The heavy construction equipment market is projected to grow at a 5.0% CAGR to $286.51 billion by 2030 (2026-03-16).
*   **Geopolitical and Macro Volatility (March 2026):** Stock performance was tied to oil price movements due to Iran tensions. CAT shares declined on **2026-03-19** amid an oil price spike to $119/barrel. Conversely, it was recommended as a beneficiary if ceasefire talks materialized, which could lower energy costs (2026-03-25). A broader market rally on **2026-03-31** followed calmer geopolitical developments.
*   **Patent Litigation (February-March 2026):** An escalating patent dispute with Doosan Bobcat is active. The U.S. International Trade Commission opened an investigation on **2026-02-24** at CAT’s request. On **2026-03-25**, Caterpillar countersued Bobcat, which had alleged CAT infringed 14 patents.
*   **Potential Tariff Risk (March 2026):** A **2026-03-15** article identifies President Trump’s proposed 15% global tariff as a potential headwind for industrial manufacturers reliant on imported materials.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Geopolitical and Commodity Sensitivity:** Exposure to oil & gas and global construction ties its fortunes to volatile energy prices and international conflicts (e.g., Iran tensions impacting oil on **2026-03-19**).
*   **Risk: Trade Policy Uncertainty:** Proposed tariffs (**2026-03-15**) could increase input costs and disrupt global supply chains, which the 10-K notes are already subject to constraint risks.
*   **Risk: Competitive and Legal Pressures:** Intense competition across all segments can lead to price discounting and margin pressure. The ongoing patent lawsuit with Doosan Bobcat (Feb/Mar 2026) represents a direct legal and operational distraction.
*   **Offset: Secular Growth in Key End Markets:** Confirmed demand drivers include the AI data center boom, global infrastructure spending, and U.S. reshoring, providing multi-year growth visibility beyond cyclical factors.
*   **Offset: Strong Backlog and Financial Position:** The reported order backlog of $51.2 billion (as of 2025-12-31) provides significant revenue visibility. The company is noted for its strong balance sheet, A+ credit rating, and 30-year dividend growth streak.

**4. Qualitative Assessment: POSITIVE**
The qualitative evidence points to a robust franchise with strong secular tailwinds. Caterpillar is strategically positioned at the intersection of several powerful, durable trends—AI infrastructure build-out, global reshoring, and energy transition—each driving demand across its diverse segments. Recent corporate actions, such as the Atlas Energy partnership, demonstrate active capitalizing on these themes. While near-term macro volatility (geopolitics, tariffs) and litigation pose risks, they appear cyclical or manageable against the company’s scale, entrenched dealer network, and strong backlog. The narrative has shifted from a pure cyclical industrial to a diversified infrastructure and technology enabler, justifying a positive outlook.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Caterpillar's strong secular tailwinds (AI data center buildout, reshoring, infrastructure) and $51.2B backlog are offset by extreme valuation (PE 37.7x near all-time highs) alongside deteriorating earnings (-11.4% YoY EPS decline) and margin compression (net margin 13.1% vs 16.6% prior year). The stock's +114.9% annual return has far outpaced fundamentals, creating a risk/reward that is roughly balanced but skewed toward downside if earnings don't reaccelerate.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Caterpillar's strong secular tailwinds (AI data center buildout, reshoring, infrastructure) and $51.2B backlog are offset by extreme valuation (PE 37.7x near all-time highs) alongside deteriorating earnings (-11.4% YoY EPS decline) and margin compression (net margin 13.1% vs 16.6% prior year). The stock's +114.9% annual return has far outpaced fundamentals, creating a risk/reward that is roughly balanced but skewed toward downside if earnings don't reaccelerate.
key_factors: Valuation stretched: PE of 37.7x and P/B of 15.7x are near the top of the past year's range (12.4x to 41.2x), despite net margin contracting from 16.6% to 13.1% and ROE declining from 55.3% to 41.7%.
Earnings deterioration: YoY Q4 EPS declined -11.4% ($5.12 vs $5.78) and Q4 operating margin fell from 18.0% to 13.9%, while the stock surged +114.9% annually—a significant disconnect.

Secular tailwinds and backlog: AI data center demand (Atlas Energy $840M partnership announced 2026-03-23), reshoring trends (40%+ increase in U.S. manufacturing plant spending since 2020), and a $51.2B order backlog provide multi-year revenue visibility.

Macro and legal headwinds: Proposed 15% global tariff risk (2026-03-15), geopolitical oil price sensitivity (stock declined 2026-03-19 on $119/barrel oil), and active patent litigation with Doosan Bobcat (ITC investigation opened 2026-02-24).

Further, we do not view the interconnected experience as a specific transaction; rather, we believe it encompasses an entire journey from inspiration and know-how, to purchase and fulfillment, to post-purchase support. Customers continue to expect more personalized messaging, and we remain focused on connecting marketing activities with the online and in-store experiences to create seamless engagement across channels. From the inspirational point of the purchase journey to providing product know-how, we continue to invest in the infrastructure and capabilities needed to deliver the most relevant marketing messages to our customers based on what is important to them today.
Store Experience
Our stores remain the hub of our business, and we continue to invest to improve the customer shopping experience through easier navigation and increased convenience and speed of checkout. In fiscal 2025, we continued to leverage the investments made in our store experience over the past several years to operate effectively and meet customer expectations, as well as investing in dedicated teams to better meet the needs of our Pros in-store. We have also empowered our customers with additional self-help tools, including mobile application-enabled store navigation and Magic Apron. Our Home Depot mobile application provides store-specific maps that allow customers to pinpoint the exact location of an item on their mobile devices. We also recognize that another critical part of delivering an excellent customer experience in the store is having products on our shelves, and we have enhanced our use of technology such as Computer Vision, which provides greater visibility into where product is located, including both on shelves and in the overhead space, enabling us to drive higher on-shelf availability. We believe these investments are driving higher customer satisfaction scores, and we will continue to invest to improve the customer experience. In addition, we have identified areas that have experienced significant population growth or where market voids exist. In fiscal 2023, we initiated a plan to open approximately 80 new stores over a five-year period to address those opportunities, and in fiscal 2025, we opened 12 new stores. We expect to complete this goal of approximately 80 new stores in fiscal 2027, after which point we plan to open approximately 15 to 20 new stores per year. These new stores will continue to help relieve pressure at existing high-volume stores and add capacity in areas with less store coverage, helping us to improve the customer experience and drive sales growth.
Investing in the Associate Experience.
We continually strive to improve our store operations by removing complexity and inefficient processes, allowing our associates to spend more of their time serving our customers. To this end, we have continued to focus on process improvements like optimizing product flow to improve on-shelf availability and reduce the amount of time store associates spend locating products, as well as expanding in-aisle, real-time mobile learning tools for our associates' development and assistance with customer questions. Our associates are also using web-enabled handheld devices to help them more efficiently meet the needs of the business and serve customers. These digital "hdPhone" devices offer enhanced functionality that allows associates to readily query inventory, access applications that support customer service, and assist with locating products. These include rapid deployment centers, stocking distribution centers, bulk distribution centers, FDCs, and direct fulfillment centers, among others. Over the past several years, we have invested to further automate and mechanize our rapid deployment center network to drive greater efficiency and faster movement of product.
We have also enhanced our supply chain network with our expanded fulfillment facilities designed to drive speed and reliability of delivery for our customers. Our 'ship from best location' initiative seeks to optimize delivery speed, reliability, cost, and capacity by leveraging machine learning algorithms to route orders to the optimal fulfillment node. In many markets, we offer same day or next day delivery of a multitude of products through our stores and fulfillment centers. HD.quantitative_report:
# HD — Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2026-04-01)

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow & Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue:** Latest Q4 (FY25, period ending 2026-02-01) revenue was **$38.20B**, down **−7.6% QoQ** from Q3's $41.35B and **−3.8% YoY** versus Q4-FY24's $39.70B. TTM revenue stands at **$159.11B**. The quarterly sequence shows a peak at Q2-FY24 ($43.17B) with a general downward drift since.

**Earnings:** Latest Q4 EPS was **$2.58**, down sharply from Q3's **$3.62** (−28.7% QoQ) and YoY Q4-FY24's $3.01 (−14.3%). TTM net income is **$12.60B**. The EPS trajectory has deteriorated from a Q2-FY24 peak of **$4.60** through a volatile but declining series to the current trough.

**Margins:** Latest Q4 gross margin compressed to **32.6%** ($12.47B / $38.20B) versus Q3's **33.4%** ($13.81B / $41.35B). Operating margin fell to **10.1%** ($3.85B / $38.20B) from Q3's **12.9%** ($5.35B / $41.35B). TTM-reported gross margin is **33.3%**, operating margin **12.7%**, net margin **8.6%** — all under pressure.

**Cash Flow:** **No cash-flow data available** for any period in the supplied evidence. Free-cash-flow yield and TTM FCF are unreportable.

**Balance Sheet:** Total assets grew to **$105.09B** from $96.12B a year prior. Total equity expanded to **$12.81B** from $6.64B (2025-02-02), though it remains thin relative to assets. Total debt is **$49.40B**, modestly reduced from **$51.37B** a year earlier. Total liabilities of **$92.28B** represent 87.8% of assets.

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality:** Net margin has contracted from **9.3%** (April–May 2025 observations) to **8.6%** (latest), a steady erosion. The latest quarter's EPS of $2.58 is the **lowest in the seven-quarter series**, while revenue is only the second-lowest ($36.42B in Q1-FY24 was lower). This indicates margin compression is accelerating, not merely a revenue-volume issue. ROE remains extremely high at **110.5%** (TTM), but this is mechanically inflated by the very small equity base ($12.81B), not by strong profitability growth.

**Balance-sheet resilience:** Debt-to-equity of **3.855** is elevated. Equity of $12.81B against $49.40B in debt means the company operates with significant financial leverage. Equity has improved sequentially from $4.42B (Jul 2024), but this recovery is partly a book-value revaluation effect rather than organic capital build, given the declining net income trend. With no cash-flow data, the ability to service debt from operations cannot be directly verified — a material evidence gap.

## 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History

| Metric | Latest (2026-03-31) | 1-Year Range | Position |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E (diluted) | **23.1** | 22.5 – 28.8 | **At floor** |
| P/B | **25.5** | 24.9 – 56.9 | **At floor** |
| P/S | **1.99** | n/a | n/a |
| Earnings yield | **4.3%** | n/a | n/a |
| Net margin | **8.6%** | 8.9% – 10% | **At floor** |
| ROE | **110.5%** | 120% – 223% | **Declining** |

The P/E of 23.1 sits at the **bottom of its 1-year range** (22.5–28.8). However, this is not a standalone value signal: the P/E is low precisely because earnings are declining, and the multiple has compressed alongside the fundamental deterioration. P/B has collapsed from ~55x to ~25.5x as equity grew but also as the stock price fell. Valuation is **at historical lows** on both P/E and P/B within the supplied window, but this coincides with the weakest earnings trajectory in the data set.

## 4. Price Level & Return Context Versus Fundamentals

- **Current price:** $328.89 (2026-03-31)
- **52-week high/low:** $426.75 / $320.26 → current price is **within 2.7% of the 52-week low**
- **Quarterly return (≈63 days):** **−5.3%** (from $347.45)
- **Annual return (≈252 days):** **−8.2%** (from $358.15)
- **Peak-to-current:** −23.0% from $426.75 (Sep 2025 area)

The price path peaked around Q3-25 ($406.80 close on 2025-09-29) and has declined in three of the last four quarters. The price decline broadly tracks the earnings deterioration: as EPS fell from $3.67 (Q3-FY24) → $2.58 (Q4-FY25), the stock fell from ~$407 to ~$329. The market has not rerated the multiple upward to compensate for lower earnings; instead, both earnings and the multiple have compressed simultaneously.

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside case (quantitative):**
- P/E at the floor of its 1-year range (23.1 vs. 28.8 top). If earnings stabilize and the multiple mean-reverts to even the midpoint (~25.7), the stock re-rates upward **~11%** without any earnings growth.
- Equity base has expanded substantially ($6.64B → $12.81B over 12 months), suggesting balance-sheet repair is ongoing.
- TTM revenue of $159.11B remains large; a single-quarter revenue beat could shift sentiment.

**Downside case (quantitative):**
- EPS trajectory: from $4.60 to $2.58 in six quarters is a **−43.9% decline**. If the trend continues, the P/E floor breaks and the stock re-rates lower.
- Debt-to-equity of 3.855 with only $12.81B in equity means a relatively small deterioration in asset values could impair equity significantly.
- No free-cash-flow data means capital return capacity and debt-servicing ability are unverifiable — a risk the market may not be fully pricing.
- Price is only 2.7% above the 52-week low; a break below $320.26 could trigger further selling.
- Operating margin has fallen to 10.1% in the latest quarter from 12.9% the prior quarter — a **280 bps sequential compression** that, if sustained, materially reduces earnings power.

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

HD presents a **conflicted** quantitative picture. The stock sits at multi-quarter valuation lows (P/E 23.1, P/B 25.5) and near its 52-week price low ($328.89 vs. $320.26), but these lows coincide with the **worst earnings trajectory** in the data set — EPS down 43.9% from peak, margins compressing, and revenue declining. The balance sheet carries substantial leverage (D/E 3.855) with an extremely thin equity cushion. The absence of cash-flow data is a critical gap that prevents assessment of whether operating cash generation supports the capital structure. On balance, the declining fundamentals justify the low valuation rather than offering a dislocation; the stock appears **fairly priced for its deterioration trajectory**, with limited evidence of an inflection. Downside risk from a further EPS decline or a break of the $320 support level is quantitatively symmetrical to the upside from a multiple reversion to the midpoint — neither scenario is supported by a clear directional catalyst in the supplied numbers.
HD.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: The Home Depot (HD)**
**Decision Date: 2026-04-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Home Depot is the world's largest home improvement retailer (10-K, 2026-03-18). Its franchise is built on a wide assortment of products and services for DIY consumers, DIFM (Do-It-For-Me) customers, and professional (Pro) contractors, supported by a massive network of 2,359 stores and a growing digital presence across North America. The business strategy is anchored in three pillars: delivering an interconnected shopping experience, winning with Pros through a dedicated ecosystem, and driving core operations and culture (10-K, 2026-03-18). A key strategic move is the 2024-2025 acquisition of SRS (including GMS), which expands its reach into specialty trade distribution for roofing, landscape, pool, and interior building products, significantly bolstering its Pro offerings (10-K, 2026-03-18). The company possesses a strong brand, a leading supply chain network, and a portfolio of proprietary brands that provide differentiation and margin benefits (10-K, 2026-03-18).

### 2. Recent Developments (Point-in-Time)
*   **Guidance & Outlook (2026-02-27):** Company guidance for 2026 points to modest sales growth of 2.5-4.5%, with near-term catalysts centered on executing Pro-focused initiatives rather than a sharp consumer rebound (Web context, 2026-02-27; 2026-03-25).
*   **Store Expansion (2026-03-25):** Management is betting on a 12-store expansion to capture Pro demand and minor repair traffic, part of a multi-year plan to open approximately 80 new stores by fiscal 2027 (Web context, 2026-03-25; 10-K, 2026-03-18).
*   **Market Sentiment & Stock Performance (2026-03-22, 2026-03-23):** HD stock is noted as being down significantly from its peaks (14-25%), presenting a potential dividend investment opportunity with a yield around 2.85%. It is recommended as a long-term "buy and hold" for its dividend growth and business fundamentals despite near-term headwinds (News, 2026-03-22; 2026-03-23).
*   **Strategic Positioning (2026-03-16, 2026-03-21):** The company is consistently cited by financial commentary as a top retail stock to hold for decades due to its wide economic moat, strong brand, cost advantages, and market dominance (News, 2026-03-16; 2026-03-21).
*   **Emerging Catalysts (2026-03-29):** Sustainability trends, including demand for energy-efficient products and smart home technologies, are identified as potential growth catalysts aligning with Home Depot's expanding assortments (Web context, 2026-03-29).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Legal & Regulatory Exposure (2026-02-22, 2026-03-11, 2026-03-23):** The company faces ongoing litigation, including class action lawsuits related to a data breach and alleged hidden fees in its tool rental damage protection plans. These pose reputational risk, potential financial penalties, and could lead to increased compliance costs (Web context, 2026-02-22; 2026-03-11; 2026-03-23).
*   **Competitive & Market Pressures (10-K, 2026-03-18):** The industry is highly competitive, fragmented, and evolving, with pressure from traditional retailers, online players, and specialty distributors. Increased price transparency and AI tools facilitate comparison shopping, potentially pressuring margins (10-K, 2026-03-18).
*   **Housing Market & Macro Sensitivity (2026-03-25):** Growth is exposed to the health of the housing market and discretionary consumer spending. Weakness in housing turnover or large-scale remodeling projects remains a risk to sales (Web context, 2026-03-25).
*   **Operational Execution (2026-02-27):** The modest growth outlook indicates that execution on strategic initiatives, particularly with Pro customers and new stores, is critical, and underperformance here is a key risk (Web context, 2026-02-27).

**Offsets:**
*   **Pro Customer Moat & SRS Acquisitions (10-K, 2026-03-18; Web context, 2026-03-29):** Deep relationships with Pros, a dedicated sales force, and the expanded capabilities from SRS/GMS create a significant competitive barrier and a less discretionary revenue stream compared to pure DIY.
*   **Scale & Supply Chain (10-K, 2026-03-18; Web context, 2026-03-29):** Unmatched scale, a sophisticated supply chain, and a premier store footprint provide cost advantages and enable the "interconnected experience" that is difficult for competitors to replicate.
*   **Shareholder Return Commitment (10-K, 2026-03-18):** A disciplined capital allocation framework prioritizing reinvestment, followed by a strong dividend and share repurchases, supports the long-term investment thesis for income-focused investors (10-K, 2026-03-18; News, 2026-03-23).

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.**
The evidence presents a balanced picture of a high-quality franchise with durable competitive advantages facing a period of modest growth and specific operational and legal challenges. The core business remains strong, with a defensible market position, strategic investments in growth areas (Pros, sustainability, store expansion), and a consistent return of capital. However, near-term growth guidance is tepid, and material risks from litigation and competition are present. The recent stock price decline reflects these headwinds, potentially creating a more attractive valuation for long-term holders, but does not eliminate the near-term uncertainty. The assessment is therefore neutral, acknowledging the company's fundamental strength while recognizing the current constrained outlook.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: HD sits at multi-quarter valuation lows (P/E 23.1 at floor of 22.5-28.8 range) near its 52-week low ($328.89 vs. $320.26), but these lows are justified by a severe earnings deterioration — EPS down 43.9% from peak to $2.58, operating margin compressed 280bps sequentially to 10.1%, and revenue declining 3.8% YoY. The balance sheet carries elevated leverage (D/E 3.855) with only $12.81B equity against $49.40B debt, and the absence of cash-flow data prevents verification of debt-servicing capacity. While the franchise remains world-class with Pro customer expansion via SRS/GMS and modest 2.5-4.5% growth guidance, the quantitative picture is one of fair pricing for deterioration rather than dislocation.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: HD sits at multi-quarter valuation lows (P/E 23.1 at floor of 22.5-28.8 range) near its 52-week low ($328.89 vs. $320.26), but these lows are justified by a severe earnings deterioration — EPS down 43.9% from peak to $2.58, operating margin compressed 280bps sequentially to 10.1%, and revenue declining 3.8% YoY. The balance sheet carries elevated leverage (D/E 3.855) with only $12.81B equity against $49.40B debt, and the absence of cash-flow data prevents verification of debt-servicing capacity. While the franchise remains world-class with Pro customer expansion via SRS/GMS and modest 2.5-4.5% growth guidance, the quantitative picture is one of fair pricing for deterioration rather than dislocation.
key_factors: P/E of 23.1 sits at the floor of its 1-year range (22.5-28.8) and price is only 2.7% above the 52-week low of $320.26, but this coincides with the worst earnings trajectory: EPS declined 43.9% from $4.60 peak to $2.58 over six quarters

Operating margin compressed 280bps sequentially to 10.1% in Q4-FY25 from 12.9% in Q3, with gross margin also declining to 32.6% from 33.4%, indicating accelerating margin pressure beyond revenue weakness

Balance sheet carries significant leverage with debt-to-equity of 3.855 ($49.40B debt vs. $12.81B equity), and no cash-flow data is available to verify operating cash generation supports the capital structure

Company guided 2026 sales growth of only 2.5-4.5% with near-term catalysts centered on Pro-focused initiatives and a 12-store expansion, while facing ongoing litigation from data breach class actions and hidden fee allegations

DIS.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Report: DIS (Walt Disney Company) as of 2026-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: TTM revenue is $95.72B. Quarterly revenue shows volatility: Q1 2025 (2025-12-27) revenue of $25.98B is up 15.7% QoQ from Q4 2025 ($22.46B) but only +5.2% YoY from Q1 2024 ($24.69B). Over eight quarters, revenue ranged from $22.08B to $25.98B.
- **Earnings**: TTM net income is $13.27B. Quarterly net income is highly variable: Q1 2025 net income of $2.48B (EPS $1.34) vs. Q3 2025 net income of $5.94B (EPS $2.92). YoY, Q1 2025 net income declined 6.1% from Q1 2024 ($2.64B).
- **Margins**: Latest net margin is 13.9% (0.139), operating margin 17.9% (0.179). Net margin improved from 6.8% in April 2025 to 13.9% in March 2026.
- **Cash-Flow**: No data available for any quarter.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets grew from $195.11B (Q2 2024) to $202.09B (Q1 2025). Total equity increased from $103.76B to $114.01B. Total liabilities decreased from $91.35B to $88.08B. Debt (available for two quarters) declined from $45.81B (Q3 2024) to $42.03B (Q3 2025).

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: High quarterly volatility in net income and EPS (range: -$0.01 to $2.92 over eight quarters) suggests lumpiness or non-recurring items. YoY EPS decline of 4.3% despite revenue growth of 5.2% indicates possible margin pressure. No cash-flow data prevents assessment of accruals or cash conversion.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity is growing and liabilities are declining. Debt-to-equity ratio (using 2025-09-27 data) is 0.367 ($42.03B debt / $114.61B equity), down from 0.434 in Q3 2024. Solvency appears stable, but liquidity and interest coverage cannot be assessed without cash-flow data.

**3. Valuation versus Company's Own History**
- Latest valuation: PE diluted = 13.022, P/B = 1.516, P/S = 1.805, earnings yield = 7.7%.
- Historical range (2025-04-01 to 2026-03-31): PE diluted ranged from 12.5 to 28.4; P/B from 1.4 to 2.1. Current PE is near the low end (12.5). P/B is also near the low end (1.4).
- Net margin improved from 6.8% to 13.9% and ROE from 5.9% to 11.6% over the same period, yet PE compressed from 28.341 to 13.022, indicating the market may be discounting future earnings or sustainability concerns.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context**
- **Price**: Latest close $96.38 (2026-03-31). 52-week range: $80.10 to $124.69.
- **Returns**: Quarterly return (from 2025-12-29) is -15.6% ($114.19 to $96.38). Annual return (from 2025-03-28) is -1.7% ($98.07 to $96.38).
- **Fundamental Context**: The -15.6% quarterly price decline occurred despite Q1 2025 revenue up 15.7% QoQ and EPS up 83.6% QoQ. However, YoY EPS declined 4.3%, which may explain the negative momentum.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**
- **Upside**: 
  - Valuation is near historical lows (PE 13.0 vs. range 12.5–28.4); earnings yield of 7.7% is high.
  - Balance sheet is improving: equity growth and debt reduction.
  - Net margin expansion from 6.8% to 13.9% over the past year.
- **Downside**:
  - Earnings volatility and YoY EPS decline (-4.3%) raise sustainability concerns.
  - Negative price momentum: -15.6% quarterly return.
  - Absence of cash-flow data limits assessment of earnings quality and financial flexibility.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
DIS trades at a low valuation relative to its recent history, with an improving balance sheet and significant margin expansion. However, earnings are volatile and showed a YoY decline in the latest quarter, and the stock has negative price momentum. The risk is that the market is pricing in lower future earnings or that margin gains are unsustainable. The reward is potential multiple expansion if earnings stabilize. Without cash-flow data, earnings quality remains uncertain. Quantitatively, the stock appears cheap but with elevated fundamental uncertainty.
DIS.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment Report: The Walt Disney Company (DIS)
**Decision Date:** 2026-04-01
**Evidence Cutoff:** 2026-03-29

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 2025-11-13 10-K)**
Disney is a diversified worldwide entertainment company operating across three segments:
*   **Entertainment:** Encompasses linear networks (ABC, Disney Channel, FX, Nat Geo), direct-to-consumer streaming (Disney+, Hulu), and content sales/licensing (theatrical, TV/VOD, home entertainment). It leverages a vast IP library (Marvel, Star Wars, Pixar, etc.).
*   **Sports:** Primarily centers on ESPN (80% owned), a leading sports media network with extensive linear channels and a growing DTC platform. It holds major sports rights (NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, etc.) and is in the process of acquiring NFL Network and other assets.
*   **Experiences:** Includes domestic and international theme parks/resorts (Walt Disney World, Disneyland Paris, etc.), cruise line, vacation club, and a global consumer products licensing and retail business.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated Evidence)**
*   **2026-03-29:** Disney's streaming segment (Disney+ and Hulu) reportedly achieved a ninefold increase in operating income to $1.3B in fiscal 2025, with projections for $2.1B in fiscal 2026.
*   **2026-03-26:** Disney's stock has fallen 15% since January 2026, attributed to CEO transition uncertainty and streaming strategy concerns.
*   **2026-03-19 / 2026-03-18:** Josh D'Amaro officially became CEO, replacing Bob Iger. Analyst commentary (Guggenheim, 2026-03-18) suggests he can leverage Disney's brands, optimize parks, and improve streaming transparency.
*   **2026-02-27:** Short-term narrative requires Disney to prove "back-end weighted" growth projections via its summer 2026 theatrical slate and new cruise ship launch.
*   **2026-02-14 (Risk Context):** A series of injunctions related to core video technologies could lead to higher licensing fees, distribution changes, or service disruptions if appeals fail.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets**
*   **Risk: CEO Transition & Strategic Uncertainty (2026-03-19, 2026-03-26):** The leadership change from Bob Iger to Josh D'Amaro introduces uncertainty about future strategy, contributing to stock volatility.
    *   **Offset (2026-03-18):** Analyst view that D'Amaro can leverage iconic IP and drive growth in parks and streaming.
*   **Risk: Competitive & Margin Pressure in Streaming (2026-03-15, 2026-03-29):** Streaming, while profitable, is still far behind Netflix's operating margins, and competition from deep-pocketed tech giants remains intense.
    *   **Offset (2026-03-29, 2026-03-19):** The segment has shown dramatic profitability improvement ($1.3B operating income in FY25) with continued growth projected, indicating a strengthening business model.
*   **Risk: IP Legal/Settlement Issues (2026-02-14, 2026-02-18):** Injunctions on video technology and a record $2.75M CCPA fine for data privacy violations present regulatory and operational risks.
    *   **Offset:** The privacy settlement (2026-02-18) is a contained financial penalty, and ongoing legal matters are presented as appealable.
*   **Risk: Execution Risk on Growth Catalysts (2026-02-27):** Meeting "back-end weighted" financial projections is contingent on successful execution of its 2026 film slate and new cruise ship launch.
    *   **Offset:** The diversified portfolio (Experiences, Sports) provides stability while Entertainment catalysts are pursued.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral with a Positive Bias.** Disney possesses a world-class portfolio of IP and diversified business segments, providing a strong foundation. The most recent narrative (early 2026) is dominated by the CEO transition, which introduces near-term uncertainty but is not inherently destructive. Simultaneously, the streaming business has undergone a tangible and rapid improvement in profitability, shifting from a drag to a potential growth driver. The primary risks are execution-oriented—proving the new leadership strategy and hitting projected growth targets—while the franchise value and recent operational momentum in streaming offer meaningful qualitative offsets. The current narrative balances strategic uncertainty against demonstrated improvement in a key segment.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Disney trades at a historically low PE of 13.0 with improving margins (6.8% to 13.9% net margin) and a strengthening balance sheet, but near-term headwinds from CEO transition uncertainty, YoY EPS decline of 4.3%, and negative price momentum (-15.6% quarterly) temper the upside case.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Disney trades at a historically low PE of 13.0 with improving margins (6.8% to 13.9% net margin) and a strengthening balance sheet, but near-term headwinds from CEO transition uncertainty, YoY EPS decline of 4.3%, and negative price momentum (-15.6% quarterly) temper the upside case.
key_factors: Valuation near historical lows: PE of 13.0 vs. 12-month range of 12.5–28.4, with earnings yield of 7.7% and P/B of 1.5 near the low end of 1.4–2.1.

Significant margin expansion: Net margin improved from 6.8% to 13.9% and ROE from 5.9% to 11.6% over the past year, yet PE compressed from 28.3 to 13.0, suggesting the market may be discounting sustainability.
Streaming profitability inflection: Disney+ and Hulu operating income surged ninefold to $1.3B in FY25 with projections of $2.1B in FY26, providing a tangible growth catalyst.

Earnings volatility and negative momentum: YoY EPS declined 4.3% despite 5.2% revenue growth, quarterly net income ranged from -$0.01 to $2.92 EPS over eight quarters, and the stock fell 15.6% in Q1 2026 amid CEO transition uncertainty.

generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. For example, as a result of the 737-9 door plug accident in January 2024, the FAA investigated the 737 quality control system, including Spirit AeroSystems Holdings, Inc. (Spirit), and increased its oversight of our production and quality and safety management systems. The 737 program may only increase production rates and/or implement new production lines with the concurrence of the FAA. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the utilization, discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to monitor compliance with applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations. For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 15 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability and quality of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs.

We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
